\section*{Insert the following sentence below Table 61:}

For transport CIDs, the Direction bits shall be set to 0 b 00 on transmission, and ignored on reception. For secondary management CIDs, the Direction bits shall be set to 0 b 01 or 0 b 10 as appropriate and other values shall cause the message to be treated as invalid and discarded on reception.

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.33 Channel measurement Report Request/Response (REP-REQ/RSP)}

\section*{Change the first paragraph as indicated:}

If the BS, operating in bands below 11 GHz or a DM-configured BS operating at any frequency, requires RSSI and CINR channel measurement reports, it shall send the channel measurements Report Request message. In license exempt bands, it The Report Request message shall additionally be used to request the results of the DFS measurements the BS has previously scheduled. Table 62 shows the REP-REQ meassage message.

\section*{Change the second paragraph as indicated:}

The REP-REQ message shall contain the following TLV encoded parameters:

\section*{Report Request}

The channel measurement Report Response message shall be used by the SS to respond to the channel measurements listed in the received Report Requests. In license-exempt bands Where regulation mandates detection of specific signals by the SS, the SS shall also send a REP-RSP in an unsolicited fashion upon detecting a Primary User such signals on the channel it is operating in, if mandated by regulatory requirements. The SS may also send a REP-RSP containing channel measurement reports, in an unsolicited fashion, or when primary user other interference is detected above a threshold value. In cases where specific signal detection by an SS is not mandated by regulation, the SS may indicate 'Unmeasured. Channel not measured" (see 11.12) in the REP-RSP message when responding to the REP-REQ message from the BS. Table 63 shows the REP-RSP message.

\section*{Insert the following sentence at the end of the subclause:}

Upon sending a REP-RSP message, an SS shall reset all its measurement counters for each channel on which it reported.

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.34 Fast Power Control (FPC) message}

\section*{Change first paragraph as indicated:}

Power control shall be effected by the use of periodic ranging. In addition, the BS may adjust the power levels of multiple subscribers simultaneously with the Fast Power Control (FPC) message. SSs shall apply the indicated change within the "SS downlink management message FPC processing time". If the SS cannot apply the commanded power correction (SS is already at maximum or minimum power) the SS shall send a RNG-REQ message with "Ranging Anomalies" parameter. FPC shall be sent on the broadcast CID. This message shall only apply to SCa, OFDM, and OFDMA PHY specifications. See Table 64. Implementation of FPC message at BS is optional.

\section*{Insert the following record into Table 64, after the 'Number of Stations' field:}

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Power measurement frame & 8 bits & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

\section*{Insert the following text below Table 64:}

\section*{Power measurement frame}

The 8 LSB of the frame number in which the BS measured the power corrections referred to in the message.

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.41 AAS Beam Select message}

\section*{Change the description of "AAS beam index" field below Table 86 as indicated:}

\section*{AAS beam index}

This index shall correspond to the direction the AAS beam is pointing at during the AAS_DL_Scan_IE() AAS_DLFP() preferred by the SS (see 8.4.4.6).

\section*{Insert the following text at the end of 6.3.2.3.41:}

For systems supporting mobility, the parameter "Allow AAS Beam Select Messages" in the UCD channel encoding TLV messages can be configured to indicate that these messages should not be sent by any MS, and the default value of "Allow AAS Beam Select Messages" is 1 .

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.42 SS De-registration Request (DREG-REQ) message}

\section*{Change 6.3.2.3.42 as indicated:}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 87-DREG-REQ message format}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline DREG-REQ message format() \{ & - & - \\
\hline Management message type = 49 & 8 bits & - \\
\hline De-Registration_Request_Code & 8 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
0x00 = SS De-Registration request from BS 0x01=request for MS De-Registration from serving BS and initiation of MS Idle Mode \\
0x02 = Response for the Unsolicited MS De-Registration initiated by the BS. \\
0x013-0xFF = Reserved
\end{tabular} \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 87-DREG-REQ message format (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline TLV encoded parameters & variable & - \\
\hline$\}$ & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

An SS shall generate SS DREG-REQs including the following parameters:

\section*{De-Registration_Request_Code}

Request code identifying the type of ADe-rRegistration request:
0x00 = SS dDe-rRegistration request for de-registration from BS and network
0x01 = MS request for dDe-fRegistration from serving BS and initiation of Idle Mode
0x02 $=$ MS response for the Unsolicited De-Registration initiated by BS
0x043-0xFF = Reserved

The DREG-REQ shall include the following parameters encoded as TLV tuples:

\section*{HMAC/CMAC Tuple (see 11.1.2)}

The HMAC/CMAC Tuple shall be the last attribute in the message.

The MS shall include the following parameters in the DREG-REQ only if De-Registration_Request_Code = 0x01:

\section*{Paging Cycle Request}

PAGING_CYCLE requested by the MS

\section*{Idle Mode Retain Information}

MS request for Paging Controller retention of network re-entry related MAC management message MS service and operational information to expedite future Network Re-entry from Idle Mode. For each Bit location, a value of 0 indicates the information associated with the specified MAC management message is not requested to be retained and managed, a value of 1 indicates the information is requested to be retained and managed.
Bit \#0: Retain MS service and operational information associated with SBC-REQ/RSP messages.
Bit \#1: Retain MS service and operational information associated with PKM-REQ/RSP messages.
Bit \#2: Retain MS service and operational information associated with REG-REQ/RSP messages.
Bit \#3: Retain MS service and operational information associated with Network Address.
Bit \#4: Retain MS service and operational information associated with Time of Day.
Bit \#5: Retain MS service and operational information associated with TFTP messages.
Bit \#6: Retain MS service and operation information associated with Full service (MAC state machines, CS classifier information, etc.). The information retained by setting Bit \#6 does not include those information associated with SBC-REQ/RSP messages, PKM-REQ/RSP messages, REG-REQ/RSP messages, Network Address, Time of Day, and TFTP messages unless otherwise specified by setting one or more Bits \#0-\#5.
Bit \#7: Consider Paging Preference of each Service Flow in resource retention. Bit \#7 is meaningful when Bit \#2 and Bit \#6 have a value of 1. If Bit \#2, Bit \#6 and Bit \#7 is 1, MS service and operational information associated with Full service (MAC state machines, CS classifier information, etc.) are retained for Service Flows with positive Paging Preference. If Bit \#2 and Bit \#6 are 1 and Bit \#7 is 0, MS service and operational information associated with Full service (MAC state machines, CS classifier information, etc.) are retained for all Service Flows.

The MS may include the following parameters in the DREG-REQ message only if DeRegistration_Request_Code $=0 \times 01$ :

\section*{MAC Hash Skip Threshold}

Maximum number of successive MOB_PAG-ADV messages that may be sent from a BS individual notification for an MS, including MS MAC address hash of an MS for which Action Code is 0b00, 'No Action Required'.

Change the title of 6.3.2.3.43 as indicated (note removal of hyphen in 'H-ARQ' throughout):

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43 H-ARQ HARQ MAP message}

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.1 \#-ARQ HARQ message format}

\section*{Change text in the second paragraph as indicated:}

BS may broadcast multiple H-ARQ HARQ MAP messages using multiple bursts after the MAP message. Each HARQ HARQ MAP message shouldshall have a different modulation and coding rate. If the frame contains DCD or UCD message following the MAP message, the H-ARQ MAP should follow DCD or UCD message.

\section*{Change the second instance of 'Compact DL-MAP IE()' field in Table 88 to 'Compact UL-MAP IE()'}

Change the text under the 'Syntax' column of the 'CRC appended' field in Table 88 from "CRC appended" to "Reserved" insert under the 'Notes' column of the same field the following sentence:

Shall be set to zero

\section*{Change the description of 'CRC appended' field below Table 88 as indicated:}

\section*{CRC appended}

A value of one indicates a CRC-32 value is appended to the end of the compressed map(s) data. The CRC is computed across all bytes of the compressed map(s) starting with the byte containing the Compressed map indicator through the last byte of the map(s) as specified by the Map message length field. The CRC calculation is the same as that used for standard MAC messages. A value of zero indicates that no CRC is appended.

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.2 Format configuration}

\section*{Change Table 91 as indicated:}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 91-Format configuration IE}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline Compact_DL-MAP_IE( ) \{ & & \\
\hline DL-MAP IE Type = 4 & 3 bits & Format_Configuration_IE \\
\hline New Format Indication & 1 bit & \begin{tabular}{l}
$0=$ Use the format configured by the latest Format_Configuration_IE \\
1 = New format
\end{tabular} \\
\hline if (New Format Indication == 1) \{ & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 91-Format configuration IE (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline CID Type & 2 bits & $$
\begin{aligned}
& \underline{0 \mathrm{~b}} 00=\text { Normal CID } \\
& \underline{0 \mathrm{~b}} 01=\text { RCID11 (default) } \\
& \underline{0 \mathrm{~b}} 10=\text { RCID7 } \\
& \underline{0 \mathrm{~b}} 11=\text { RCID3 }
\end{aligned}
$$ \\
\hline Safety Pattern & 405 bits & \\
\hline Subchannel type for Band AMC & 2 bits & $$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { See Band AMC specification (8.4.6.3). } \\
& \underline{0 \mathrm{~b}} 00=\text { Default type (default) } \\
& \underline{0 \mathrm{~b}} 01=1=1 \underline{\mathrm{bin}} \times 6 \text { symbols type } \\
& \underline{0 \mathrm{~b}} 10=2 \underline{\mathrm{bin}} \times 3 \text { symbols type } \\
& \underline{0 \mathrm{~b}} 11=3 \underline{\underline{\mathrm{bin}}} \times 2 \text { symbols type }
\end{aligned}
$$ \\
\hline Max Logical Bands & 2 bits & $$
\begin{aligned}
& 0=3 \text { bands }, \\
& 1=6 \text { bands }, \\
& 2=12 \text { bands }(\text { default }) \\
& 3=24 \text { bands }
\end{aligned}
$$ \\
\hline No. Symbols for Broadcast & $4 \underline{5}$ bits & No. Symbol, (default = 0) \\
\hline No. Symbols for DL Band AMC & $4 \underline{6}$ bits & No. Symbol, $($ default $=0)$ \\
\hline No. Symbols for UL Band AMC & $4 \underline{6}$ bits & No. Symbol, $($ default $=0)$ \\
\hline \} & & \\
\hline \} & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\section*{Change the description of the 'New Format Indication', 'Safety Pattern', 'No. Symbols for Broadcast', 'No. Symbols for DL Band AMC' and 'No. Symbols for UL Band AMC' fields below Table 91 as indicated:}

\section*{New Format Indication}

If this value set to 0 , the format should be configured by the latest Format Configuration_IE in the previous frames. Otherwise, all parameters in Format Configuration IE shouldshall be configured. The configured parameters are valid for the following Compact_DL/UL_MAP_IE. At the start of each frame all parameters are set to default values.

\section*{Safety Pattern}

If this value is less than 16, the number of safety bins is 12 and the indices of allocated bins for safety are $16 m+x$, where $x$ is the value of Safety Pattern and $m=0 \ldots 11$. If this value is not less thanfrom 16 to 31, the number of safety bins is 24 and the indices of allocated bins for safety are $16 m+x^{\prime}$ and $16 m+\left(x^{\prime}+8\right)$, where $x^{\prime}=x-16$ and $m=0 \ldots 11$. If the safety pattern exists, it should be always allocated first. The safety pattern is valid in the region of AMC zone only. If safety pattern is set to all ones, safety channel is disabled.

\section*{No. Symbols for Broadcast}

This specifies the number of symbols allocated for Broadeast subchannel.This field specifies the number of symbols allocated for Broadcast symbol region. The Broadcast symbols shall be allocated at the end of the DL sub-frame. The number of symbols is counted from the last symbol of the DL sub-frame. The PermBase for this broadcast symbol region shall be set to 0 .

\section*{No. Symbols for DL Band AMC}

This specifies the number of symbols allocated for DL Band AMC subchannel-symbol region. The symbols for Band AMC shall be allocated before the broadcast symbol region. The other DL symbols excluding the symbols for Broadcast and DL Band are allocated for the DL Normal subchannel. PermBase for DL Band AMC is the same as one for normal subchannels region.

\section*{No. Symbols for UL Band AMC}

This specifies the number of symbols allocated for UL Band AMC subchannel symbol region. The symbols for UL Band AMC shall be allocated at the end of the UL sub-frame and the number of symbols are counted from the last symbol of the UL sub-frame. The other UL symbols excluding the symbols for UL Band are allocated for the UL Normal subchannel. PermBase for UL Band AMC is the same as one for normal subchannels region.

\section*{Insert the following text before Table 92:}

A logical band is a grouping of the AMC bands defined in 8.4.6.3. For example, three logical bands imply that logical band 0 is composed of AMC bands $0 . .15$, logical band 1 is composed of AMC bands $16 . .31$, and logical band 2 is composed of AMC bands $32 \ldots 47$. In general, if $K=$ Max Logical Bands, then logical band $J=0 \ldots(K-1)]$ contains physical bands $48 / K \times J, 48 / K \times J+1, \ldots, 48 / K \times(J+1)-1$.

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.3 Reduced CID}

\section*{Change the first paragraph as indicated:}

Figure 93 Table 93 presents the format of reduced CID. BS may use reduced CID instead of basic CID or multicast CID to reduce the size of HARQ MAP message. The type of reduced CID is determined by BS considering the range of basic CIDs of SS connected with the BS and specified by the RCID_Type field of the Format Configuration IE.

\section*{Change the second paragraph as indicated:}

The reduced CID is composed of 1 bit of prefix and n -bits of LSB of CID of SS. The prefix is set to 1 for the broadcast CID or multicast polling CID and set to 0 for basic CID. The reduced CID cannot be used instead of transport CID, primary management CID, or secondary management CID. An exception to the above is when the multicast polling RCID is used in DL. If a downlink CID decoded from a prefix 1 and RCID-11 is in the range of the Multicast polling CID ( $0 \mathrm{xFF} 00-0 \mathrm{xFFFD}$ ) then the DL CID should be interpreted as a DL transport CID by subtracting 0xFF (0xFFFD-0xFEFE).

\section*{Change the third paragraph as indicated:}

Figure 22 shows the decoding of reduced CID when the RCID_Type is se 3 RCID 11 is used.
In Figure 22, replace all instances of "RDC11" with "RCID 11".
Change the description of the 'RCID n' field below Table 93 as indicated:

\section*{RCID n}
n-bits LSB of CID. If the DL CID decoded from a prefix 1 and RCID-11 is in the range of the Multicast polling CID ( $0 \mathrm{xFF} 00-0 \mathrm{xFFFD}$ ) then the DL CID should be interpreted as a DL transport CID by subtracting 0 xFF ( $0 \mathrm{xFFFD}-0 \mathrm{xFEFE}$ ).

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.4 H-ARQ HARQ control IE}

\section*{Change 6.3.2.3.43.4 as indicated (note removal of hyphen in 'H-ARQ' throughout):}

The format of HARQHARQ_Control_IE, which includes encoding/decoding information for HARQHARQ enabled DL/UL bursts, is presented in the MAC frame. This IE shall be located in the compact DL/UL MAP_IE.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 94-H-ARQHARQ Control IE format}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline H-ARQHARQ_Control_IE () & - & - \\
\hline Prefix & 1 bit & \begin{tabular}{l}
0 = Temporary disable H-ARQHARQ \\
1 = enable H-ARQHARQ
\end{tabular} \\
\hline if (Prefix == 1) \{ & - & - \\
\hline AI_SN & 1 bit & H-ARQHARQ ID Seq. No \\
\hline SPID/Reserved & 2 bits & Subpacket ID when IR is defined by the FEC mode, otherwise reserved (encoded 0b00) \\
\hline ACID & 4 bits & H-ARQHARQ CH ID \\
\hline \} else \{ & - & - \\
\hline Reserved & 3 bits & Shall be set to zero \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\section*{Prefix}

Indicates whether H-ARQHARQ is enabled or not.

\section*{AI_SN}

Defines ARQ Identifier Sequence Number. This is toggled between 0 and 1 on successfully transmitting each encoder packet with the same ARQ channel.

\section*{SPID}

Defines SubPacket ID, which is used to identify the four subpackets generated from an encoder packet. The SPID field only applies to FEC modes supporting incremental redundancy.

\section*{ACID}

Defines HARQHARQ Channel ID, which is used to identify HARQHARQ channels. Each connection can have multiple HARQ channels, each of which may have an encoder packet transaction pending.

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.5 CQICH Control IE}

\section*{Change the first paragraph as indicated:}

The format of CQICH Control IE is presented in Table 95. The specific reporting value shall follow the directions indicated in the latest CQICH allocation IE (8.4.5.4.12).

\section*{Change Table 95 as indicated:}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 95-H-ARQCQICH_Control IE format-}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline CQICH_Control_IE() \{ & - & - \\
\hline CQICH Indicator & 1 bit & If the indicator is set to 1 , the CQICH_Control IE follows. \\
\hline if (CQICH indicator == 1) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Allocation Index & 6 bits & Index to the channel in a frame the CQI report should be transmitted by the MS. \\
\hline Period (p) & 2 bits & A CQI feedback is transmitted on the CQI channels indexed by the (CQI Channel Index) by the MS in every $2^{\mathrm{p}}$ frames. \\
\hline Frame offset & 3 bits & The MS starts reporting at the frame of which the number has the same 3 LSB as the specified frame offset. If the current frame is specified, the MS should start reporting in eight frames. \\
\hline Duration (d) & 4 bits & A CQI feedback is transmitted on the CQI channels indexed by the (CQI Channel Index) by the MS for $2^{\text {(d-I) }}$ frames. If d is 0b1111, the MS should report until the BS commands the MS to stop. \\
\hline \} else \{ & - & - \\
\hline Reserved COI reporting threshold & 3 bits & Shall be zero. A threshold used by an MS to report its CINR using CQI channel; If 0b000, this threshold is neglected. SS shall treat these bits as reserved and shall be set to zero. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

Insert the following definition to the end of the subclause (following "Duration" definition):

\section*{COI reporting threshold}

Used by the MS to determine whether it reports current channel measurement through CQI channel if allocated in the future. If the value is set to 0 b 000 , this threshold is inactive afterwards; otherwise it is activated. SS shall treat these bits as reserved and shall be set to zero.

\section*{Insert the following new table:}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 95a-Threshold Values}
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline \begin{tabular}{c} 
Value \\
(binary)
\end{tabular} & CINR (dB) \\
\hline 000 & CQT inactivated \\
\hline 001 & -6.0 \\
\hline 010 & -4.0 \\
\hline 011 & -2.0 \\
\hline 100 & -0.0 \\
\hline 101 & 2.0 \\
\hline 110 & 4.0 \\
\hline 111 & 6.0 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.6 Compact_DL-MAP IE}

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.6.1 Compact_DL-MAP IE for normal subchannel}

\section*{Change the first paragraph as indicated:}

The format of Compact DL-MAP IE for normal subchannel is presented in Table 96. The direction of slot allocation for downlink is along with the subchannel index first and then the symbol index. The direction of data mapping shall be according to 8.4.3.4.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 96-H-ARQHARQ Compact_DL-MAP IE format for normal subchannel}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline Compact_DL-MAP_IE () \{ & - & - \\
\hline DL-MAP Type =0 & 3 bits & - \\
\hline UL-MAP append & 1 bit & - \\
\hline RCID_IE & variable & - \\
\hline if $($ HARQ mode $=0)$ \{ & - & - \\
\hline $\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{E P}}$ code & 4 bits & Code of encoder packet bits (see 8.4.9.2.3.5) \\
\hline $\boldsymbol{N}_{\boldsymbol{S} \boldsymbol{H} \boldsymbol{H}} \boldsymbol{\text { code }}$ & 4 bits & Code of allocated subchannels (see 8.4.9.2.3.5) \\
\hline \} else if (HARQ mode = 1) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Shortened DIUC & 3 bits & Shortened DIUC \\
\hline Companded SC & 5 bits & Code of allocated subchannels (see 8.4.9.5) \\
\hline $\}$ & - & - \\
\hline HARQHARQ_Control_IE & variable & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 96-H-ARQHARQ Compact_DL-MAP IE format for normal subchannel (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline CQICH_Control_IE & variable & - \\
\hline if (UL-MAP append) \{ & - & - \\
\hline if (HARQ mode $=0$ ) \{ & - & CTC IR \\
\hline $\boldsymbol{N}_{\boldsymbol{E} \boldsymbol{P}}$ code for UL & 4 bits & Code of encoder packet bits (see 8.4.9.2.3.5) \\
\hline $\boldsymbol{N}_{\boldsymbol{S} \boldsymbol{C} \mathbf{H}}$ code for UL & 4 bits & Code of allocated subchannels (see 8.4.9.2.3.5) \\
\hline \}else if (HARQ mode = 1) \{ & - & Generic Chase \\
\hline Shortened UIUC & $\underline{3}$ bits & Shortened UIUC \\
\hline Companded SC & $\underline{5}$ bits & Code of allocated subchannels (see 8.4.9.5) \\
\hline $\downharpoonright$ & - & - \\
\hline HARQHARQ_Control_IE for UL & variable & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\

\end{table}

\section*{DL-MAP Type}

This The DL-MAP Type value specifies the type of the Ceompact_DL-MAP IE. A value of 0 indicates the Normal Subchannel.

\section*{UL-MAP append}

A value of 1 indicates the uplink access information is appended to the end of the DL-MAP IE.

\section*{RCID_IE}

Represent the assignment of the IE.
$\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{E P}}$ code, $\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{S C H}}$ code
The combination of $N_{E P}$ code and $N_{S C H}$ code indicates the number of allocated subchannels and scheme of coding and modulation for the DL burst.

\section*{Shortened DIUC}

A shortened version of the DIUC. The shortened DIUC takes on values $0 . .7$ of the DIUC as defined in the DCD. See 8.4.5.3.1.

\section*{Companded SC}

The Companded SC indicates the number of allocated subchannels.

\section*{$\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{E P}}$ code for UL, $\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{S C H}}$ code for UL}

The combination of $N_{E P}$ code and $N_{S C H}$ code indicates the number of allocated subchannels and scheme of coding and modulation for the UL burst.

\section*{Shortened UIUC}

A shortened version of the UIUC. The shortened UIUC takes on values $1 . .8$ of the UIUC as defined in the UCD. See 8.4.5.4.1.

\section*{Companded SC}

The Companded SC indicates the number of allocated subchannels.

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.6.2 Compact_DL-MAP IE for Band AMC Subchannel}

\section*{Change the first paragraph as indicated:}

Slots for downlink AMC zone are allocated along the subchannel index first within a band. The direction of data mapping for downlink AMC zone slots shall be frequency first (across bands when multiple bands are allocated). The format of Compact DL-MAP IE for Band AMC Subchannel is presented in Table 97.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 97-H-ARQ HARQ Compact_DL-MAP IE format for band AMC}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline Compact_DL-MAP_IE () \{ & - & - \\
\hline DL-MAP Type =1 & 3 bits & - \\
\hline Reserved & 1 bit & Shall be set to zero \\
\hline RCID_IE & variable & - \\
\hline if (HARQ mode $=0$ ) \{ & - & CTC IR \\
\hline $\boldsymbol{N}_{\boldsymbol{E} \boldsymbol{P}}$ code & 4 bits & Code of encoder packet bits (see 8.4.9.2.3.5) \\
\hline $\boldsymbol{N}_{\boldsymbol{S} \boldsymbol{C} \boldsymbol{H}} \boldsymbol{c o d e}$ & 4 bits & Code of allocated subchannels (see 8.4.9.2.3.5) \\
\hline \} else if (HARQ mode = 1) \{ & - & Generic Chase \\
\hline Shortened DIUC & 3 bits & Shortened DIUC \\
\hline Companded SC & 5 bits & Code of allocated subchannels (see 8.4.9.5) \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline Nband & Nb-Band & Number of bands, $0=$ use BITMAP instead \\
\hline if ( $\mathrm{Nband}==0$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Band BITMAP & Nb-BITMAP & $n$-th LSB is 1 if $n$-th band is selected \\
\hline \} else \{ & - & - \\
\hline for ( $\mathrm{i}=0 ; \mathrm{i}<$ Nband ; $\mathrm{i}++$ ) & - & - \\
\hline Band Index & Nb-Index & Band selection. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline Allocation Mode & 2 bits & Indicates the subchannel allocation mode. 0b00 = same number of subchannels for the selected bands 0b01 = different number of subchannels for the selected bands 0b10 = total number of subchannels for the selected bands determined by $N_{S C H}$ code and $N_{E P}$ code 0b11 $=$ Reserved \\
\hline Reserved & 2 bits & Shall be set to zero \\
\hline if (Allocation Mode == 0b00)\{ & - & - \\
\hline No. Subchannels & 8 bits & 一 \\
\hline \} else if (Allocation Mode == 0b01) \{ & - & - \\
\hline for ( $\mathrm{i}=0 ; \mathrm{i}<$ band count; $\mathrm{i}++$ ) & - & If Nband is 0, band count is the number of 1 in Band BITMAP. Otherwise band count is Nband. \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\section*{Table 97-H-ARQ HARQ Compact_DL-MAP IE format for band AMC (continued)}

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline No. Subchannels & 8 bits & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline HARQ_Control_IE & variable & - \\
\hline CQICH_Control_IE & variable & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

\section*{DL-MAP Type}

This The DL-MAP Type value specifies the type of the Compact DL-MAP IE. A value of 1 indicates the Band AMC Subchannel.

\section*{RCID_IE}

Represent the assignment of the IE.
$\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{E P}}$ code, $\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{S C H}}$ code
The combination of $N_{E P}$ code and $N_{S C H}$ code indicates the number of allocated subchannels and scheme of coding and modulation for the DL burst.

\section*{Shortened DIUC}

A shortened version of the DIUC. The shortened DIUC takes on values $0 . .7$ of the DIUC as defined in the DCD. See 8.4.5.3.1.

\section*{Companded SC}

The Companded SC indicates the number of allocated subchannels.

\section*{Nband}

Indicates the number of bands selected for the burst. If this value is set to 0 , the Band BITMAP is used to indicate the number and the position of selected bands instead. The number of the maximum logical bands determines the length of this field.

\section*{Band BITMAP}

This The Band BITMAP is valid when Nband is 0 . The $n$-th LSB of the Band BITMAP is set to 1 when the $n$-th logical band is selected for the burst. If the number of the maximum logical bands is 12 then the length of the Band BITMAP is 12 bits. The band count is set to the number of 1 s in the Band BITMAP. The number of the maximum logical bands determines the length of this field.

\section*{Band Index}

This The Band Index value indexes the selected band offset and is valid when Nband is larger than 0 . The number of the maximum logical bands determines the length of this field.

\section*{Allocation Mode}

This The Allocation Mode value indicates the subchannel allocation mode in the selected bands. The value is set to binary 000 when the same numbers of subchannels are allocated in the selected bands by the following field 'No. Subchannels'. The value is set to 0 b 01 when different numbers of subchannels are allocated in each of the selected bands by the following fields 'No. Subchannels'. The value is set to 0 b 10 when the total number of subchannels allocated in the selected bands is defined by $N_{S C H}$ code and $N_{E P}$ code. The subchannels fill from the bands with lowest index.

\section*{No. Subchannels}

This The No. Subchannels value indicates the number of subchannels allocated for this burst.

\section*{Delete Figure 23-Subchannel allocation modes of Compact DL-MAP_IE for Band AMC.}

\section*{DL-MAP Type}

This The DL-MAP Type value specifies the type of the Ceompact_DL-MAP IE. A value of 2 indicates the Safety Subchannel.

\section*{RCID_IE}

Represent the assignment of the IE.

\section*{$\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{E P}}$ code, $\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{S C H}}$ code}

The combination of $N_{E P}$ code and $N_{S C H}$ code indicates the number of allocated subchannels and scheme of coding and modulation for the DL burst.

\section*{Shortened DIUC}

A shortened version of the DIUC. The shortened DIUC takes on values $0 . .7$ of the DIUC as defined in the DCD. See 8.4.5.3.1.

\section*{Companded SC}

The Companded SC indicates the number of allocated subchannels.

\section*{BIN Offset}

The offset of the BIN allocated for this DL burst. See appropriate specification.
$\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{E P}}$ code for UL, $\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{S C H}}$ code for UL
The combination of $N_{E P}$ code and $N_{S C H}$ code indicates the number of allocated subchannels and scheme of coding and modulation for the UL burst.

\section*{Shortened UIUC}

A shortened version of the UIUC. The shortened UIUC takes on values $1 . .8$ of the UIUC as defined in the UCD. See 8.4.5.4.1.

\section*{Companded SC}

The Companded SC indicates the number of allocated subchannels.

\section*{BIN Offset for UL}

The offset of the BIN allocated for this UL burst. See appropriate specification.

\section*{Insert the following text below Table 99:}

\section*{Repetition coding indication}

Indicates the repetition code used inside the allocated burst.

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.6.5 Compact DL-MAP IE for HARQ ACK BITMAP}

\section*{Change the second paragraph as indicated:}

For example, when an SS transmits a HARQ HARQ enabled burst at $i$-th frame and the burst is $j$-th HARQ HARQ enabled burst in the MAP, the SS should receive H-ARQ HARQ ACK at $j$-th LSB bit of the BITMAP, which is sent by the BS at $i+$ (frame offset)-th frame. If the HARQ ACK BITMAP is omitted, the HARQ enabled SS shall retain the transmitted HARQ burst and retransmit it when the BS requests retransmission with HARQ Control IE.

Change the description of the "BITMAP" field below Table 100 as indicated:

\section*{BITMAP}

Includes H ARQ HARQ ACK information for HARQ HARQ enabled UL bursts. The size of BITMAP should be equal or larger than the number of H-ARQ HARQ enabled UL-bursts. The $j$-th HARQ enabled burst in the UL-MAP is corresponding to the $j$-th LSB in the BITMAP.

\section*{Insert the following text at the end of 6.3.2.3.43.6.5:}

Whenever HARQ enabled UL-SDMA allocations are made within a frame, the ACK BITMAP Length shall be large enough to carry the ACKs for the both the SDMA and non-SDMA allocations. Also, the ACKs for the SDMA users allocated on the second layer shall be appended to the ACKs for the non-SDMA and firstlayer SDMA users.

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.6.6 Compact DL-MAP IE for extension}

\section*{Insert the following table at the end of 6.3.2.3.43.6.6:}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 101a-DL-MAP Subtypes}
\begin{tabular}{|c|l|}
\hline \begin{tabular}{c} 
DL-MAP \\
subtype
\end{tabular} & \multicolumn{1}{|c|}{ Description } \\
\hline 0 & TimeDiversity_MBS \\
\hline 1 & HARQ mode switch \\
\hline $2-31$ & Reserved \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\section*{Insert new subclause 6.3.2.3.43.6.7:}

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.6.7 MIMO Compact_DL_MAP IE format}

When MIMO enabled DL burst are present within a frame, they shall be allocated before non-MIMO DL burst in both diversity and AMC zones. Figure 23a exemplifies the DL HARQ subframe structure, where the optional MIMO midamble is shown and $2 \times 3$ AMC type is depicted. Both MIMO diversity and MIMO AMC zones shall contain even number of symbols. For any remaining physical resources for each zone, padding subchannels shall be allocated to the region.

\begin{figure}
\includegraphics[alt={},max width=\textwidth]{https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/a227cdd5-cf57-46a1-bc9f-bb964def4187-136.jpg?height=693&width=1400&top_left_y=1385&top_left_x=373}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Figure 23a-Downlink HARQ subframe structure}
\end{figure}

Each MIMO enabled DL burst shall be first allocated by the regular Compact_DL-MAP IE for diversity subchannels (see Table 94) and AMC subchannels (see Table 95), followed by the extended MIMO Compact_DL-MAP IE. The format of MIMO Compact_DL-MAP IE is presented in Table 101b. This extended IE shall follow right after the basic allocation IE for each MIMO enabled DL burst.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 101b—MIMO Compact_DL-MAP IE}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline MIMO_Compact_DL-MAP_IE() \{ & - & - \\
\hline Compact DL-MAP Type & 3 bits & Type $=7$ \\
\hline DL-MAP Sub-type & 5 bits & MIMO = 0x01 \\
\hline Length & 4 bits & Length of the IE in Bytes \\
\hline Mode Change & 1 bit & \begin{tabular}{l}
Indicates change of MIMO mode \\
0b0 = No change from previous allocation \\
0b1 = Change of MIMO mode
\end{tabular} \\
\hline Antenna Grouping/Selection & 1 bit & \begin{tabular}{l}
Application of antenna grouping/selection to the burst \\
0b0 = Not applied \\
0b1 = AG/AS applied
\end{tabular} \\
\hline Codebook based Precoding & 1 bit & \begin{tabular}{l}
Application of codebook based precoding to the burst \\
0b0 = Not applied \\
0b1 = Codebook based precoding applied
\end{tabular} \\
\hline N_layer & 2 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
Number of multiple coding/modulation layers \\
0b00-1 layer \\
0b01-2 layers \\
0b10-3 layers \\
0b11-4 layers
\end{tabular} \\
\hline if( Mode Change == 1) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Mt & 2 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
Indicates number of STC output streams \\
$0 \mathrm{~b} 00=1$ stream \\
0b01=2 streams \\
0b10 = 3 streams \\
0b11 = 4 streams
\end{tabular} \\
\hline If (N_Layer == 0b00) \{ & - & - \\
\hline If $(\mathrm{Mt}==0 \mathrm{~b} 01)\{$ & - & - \\
\hline Matrix & 2 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
Indicates transmission matrix (See 8.4.8) \\
0b00= Matrix A (Transmit Diversity) \\
0b01 = Matrix C (Vertical Encoding) \\
0b10-11 = Reserved
\end{tabular} \\
\hline \} elseif ( $\mathrm{Mt}==0 \mathrm{~b} 10 \| \mathrm{Mt}==10 \mathrm{~b} 1$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Matrix & 2 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
Indicates transmission matrix (See 8.4.8) \\
0b00= Matrix A(Transmit Diversity) \\
0b01 = Matrix B (Vertical Encoding) \\
0b10 = Matrix C (Vertical Encoding) \\
0b11 = Reserved
\end{tabular} \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} elseif (N_Layer == 0b01) \{ & - & - \\
\hline If $(\mathrm{Mt}==01)\{$ & - & - \\
\hline Matrix & 2 bits & - \\
\hline \} elseif ( $\mathrm{Mt}==10| | \mathrm{Mt}==11$ )\{ & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 101b—MIMO Compact_DL-MAP IE (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline Matrix & 2 bits & Indicates transmission matrix (See 8.4.8) 0b00 = Matrix B (Horizontal Encoding) 0b01-11 = Reserved \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} elseif (N_Layer == 0b10) \{ & - & - \\
\hline if $(\mathrm{Mt}==10| | \mathrm{Mt}==0 \mathrm{~b} 11)\{$ & - & - \\

\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} elseif (N_Layer == 0b11) \{ & - & - \\
\hline if $(\mathrm{Mt}==0 \mathrm{~b} 11)\{$ & - & - \\
\hline Matrix & 2 bits & Indicates transmission matrix (See 8.4.8) 0b00 = Matrix C (Horizontal Encoding) 0b01-11 = Reserved \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline if (Antenna Grouping/Selection == 1) \{ & - & - \\
\hline If (N_Layer $==0 \mathrm{~b} 00$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline If $(\mathrm{Mt}==0 \mathrm{~b} 10)\{$ & - & - \\
\hline Antenna Grouping/Selection Index & 4 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
Indicates the index of antenna grouping/ selection. See 8.4.8.3.4 and 8.4.8.3.5 0b0000 = A1 \\
0b0001 = A2 (Vertical Encoding) \\
0b0010 = A3 (Vertical Encoding) \\
0b0011 = B1 (Vertical Encoding) \\
0b0100= B2 (Vertical Encoding) \\
0b0101 = B3 (Vertical Encoding) \\
0b0110-111 = Reserved
\end{tabular} \\
\hline \} elseif ( $\mathrm{Mt}==11$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Antenna Grouping/Selection Index & 4 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
Indicates the index of antenna grouping/ selection. See 8.4.8.3.4 and 8.4.8.3.5 \\
0b0000 = A1 \\
0b0001 = A2 \\
0b0010 = A3 \\
0b0011 = B1 (Vertical Encoding) \\
0b0100= B2 (Vertical Encoding) \\
0b0101 = B3 (Vertical Encoding) \\
0b0110 = B4 (Vertical Encoding) \\
0b0111 = B5 (Vertical Encoding) \\
0b1000 = B6 (Vertical Encoding) \\
0b1001-111 = Reserved
\end{tabular} \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} elseif (N_Layer == 0b01) \{ & - & - \\
\hline If $(\mathrm{Mt}==0 \mathrm{~b} 10)\{$ & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 101b—MIMO Compact_DL-MAP IE (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline Antenna Grouping/Selection Index & 4 bits & Indicates the index of antenna grouping/ selection. See 8.4.8.3.4 and 8.4.8.3.5 0b0000= B1 (Horizontal Encoding) 0b0001 = B2 (Horizontal Encoding) 0b0010 = B3 (Horizontal Encoding) 0b0011-111 = Reserved \\
\hline \} elseif ( $\mathrm{Mt}==0 \mathrm{~b} 11$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Antenna Grouping/Selection Index & 4 bits & Indicates the index of antenna grouping/ selection. See 8.4.8.3.4 and 8.4.8.3.5 0b0000= B1 (Horizontal Encoding) 0b0001 = B2 (Horizontal Encoding) 0b0010 = B3 (Horizontal Encoding) 0b0011 = B4 (Horizontal Encoding) 0b0100= B5 (Horizontal Encoding) 0b0101 = B6 (Horizontal Encoding) 0b0110-111 = Reserved \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\

\hline \} & - & - \\

\hline if (HARQ Mode =CTC Incremental Redundancy) \{ & 4 bits & HARQ Mode is specified in the HARQ Compact_DL-MAP IE format for Switch HARQ Mode.\} \\
\hline $\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{E P}}$ & 4 bits & - \\
\hline \} Else if (HARQ Mode = Generic Chase) \{ & - & - \\

\hline Allocation Index ${ }^{1}$ & 6 bits & Index to CQICH assigned to this layer \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline if (CQICH indicator == 1) \{ & 2 bits & The number of additional CQICHs allocated to this MS. (0-3) \\
\hline CQICH_Num & 2 bits & The number of additional CQICHs allocated to this MS. (0-3) \\
\hline

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 101b-MIMO Compact_DL-MAP IE (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline for ( $\mathrm{i}=0$; $\mathrm{i}<$ CQICH_Num; $\mathrm{i}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Feedback_type & 3 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
Type of contents on the additional CQICH from MS \\
0b000 = Fast DL measurement/Default \\
Feedback with antenna grouping 0b001 = Fast DL measurement/Default \\
Feedback with antenna selection 0b010 = Fast DL measurement/Default \\
Feedback with reduced codebook 0b011 = Quantized precoding weight feedback \\
0b100 = Index to precoding matrix in codebook \\

\end{tabular} \\
\hline Allocation index & 6 bits & - \\
\hline CQICH Usage & 3 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
Indicates the usage of this CQICH \\
0b000= 6-bit CQI (default) \\
0b001 = Reserved \\
0b010 = 3-bit CQI (even) \\
0b011 = 3-bit CQI(odd) \\
0b100 = 6-bit CQI (primary) \\
0b101 = 6-bit CQI (secondary) \\

\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline Padding & variable & Padding to byte; shall be set to 0 \\

\end{table}

\section*{Matrix Indicator}

This field indicates MIMO matrix for the burst.

\section*{Antenna Grouping/Selection Index}

The 'Antenna Grouping/Selection Index' field indicates antenna grouping/selection index for the current burst. For the actual description of the following matrices, see 8.4.8.3.4 and 8.4.8.3.5.

\section*{Allocation Index ${ }^{1}$}

Indicates position from the start of the CQICH region.
The Feedback type of this CQICH shall be one of the three default types (type 0b000, 0b001, 0b010) according to the following rule:
Feedback type $=0 \mathrm{~b} 000$ if ((Antenna Grouping/Selection $==1$ ) and (matrix $==\mathrm{A}$ or B))
Feedback type $=0 \mathrm{~b} 001$ if ((Antenna Grouping/Selection ==1) and (matrix==C))
Feedback type $=0 \mathrm{~b} 010$ if ((Codebook based precoding $==1)$ )

\section*{Feedback Type}

Indicates the type of feedback content on the allocated CQICH from MS. Its mapping shall be
0b000 = Fast DL measurement/Default Feedback with antenna grouping
0b001 $=$ Fast DL measurement/Default Feedback with antenna selection
0b010 = Fast DL measurement/Default Feedback with reduced codebook
0b011 = Quantized precoding weight feedback
0b100= Index to precoding matrix in codebook
0b101 = Channel Matrix Information
0b110~0b111 = Reserved
When the feedback type is either $0 \mathrm{~b} 000,0 \mathrm{~b} 001$, or 0 b 010 , the MS shall transmit either the regular $\mathrm{S} / \mathrm{N}$ measurement using the formula in 8.4.5.4.10.5 in its lower 32 codewords in 8.4.5.4.10.4, or the MIMO mode feedback of the specified type in its upper 32 codewords according to Table 296d in 8.4.5.4.10.7.

For each layer, a codeword shall be constructed according to 8.4.9.2.3.5 with the $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{EP}}$ and $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{SCH}}$ combination and mapped onto the corresponding layer. Multiple codewords from multiple layers shall be interpreted as one HARQ channel whose parameters are given in the preceding Compact DL-MAP IE.

At the receiver, an ACK shall be transmitted only when there is no CRC error detected on every layer. Otherwise, a NACK shall be transmitted.

SDMA transmissions may be allocated on the downlink with the SDMA Compact DL-MAP IE (Table 101c). Num_layer means the number of SDMA layers (2, 3, or 4) being allocated. For each SDMA layer, if the dedicated pilot bit is set to 1 in the STC_ZONE IE (8.4.5.3.4) for the zone in which the SDMA allocations are being made, Num_layer selects the pilot format for the burst by interpreting Num_layer as the number of transmit antennas (as defined in 8.4.8), and the MS with the first RCID shall be assigned the pilot pattern corresponding to antenna 1 , of 8.4 .8 , the second to the pilot pattern corresponding to antenna 2 , and so on.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 101c—SDMA Compact_DL-MAP IE format}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline SDMA_Compact_DL-MAP_IE() \{ & - & - \\
\hline Compact DL-MAP Type & 3 bits & Type $=7$ \\
\hline DL-MAP sub-type & 5 bits & SDMA = 0x03 \\
\hline Length & 4 bits & Length of the IE in bytes \\
\hline Num_layers & 2 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
Number of multiple coding/modulation layers: \\
$0 \mathrm{~b} 00=1$ layer \\
0b01=2 layers \\
0b10 = 3 layers \\
0b11 = 4 layers
\end{tabular} \\
\hline Padding & 2 bits & Shall be set to zero. \\
\hline for ( $\mathrm{j}=1$; j<Num_layer; j++) \{ & - & This loop specifies the $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{EP}}$ for layers 2 and above when required for STC. The same $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{SCH}}$ and RCID applied for each layer \\
\hline RCID & variable & MS identifier for the current layer of the SDMA allocation \\
\hline if (HARQ Mode =CTC Incremental Redundancy) $\left\{\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{E P}}\right\}$ elseif (HARQ Mode $=$ Generic Chase) $\{$ DIUC $\}$ & 4 bits & HARQ Mode is specified in the HARQ Compact_DL-MAP IE format for Switch HARQ Mode \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 101c—SDMA Compact_DL-MAP IE format (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline CQI Feedback_type & 3 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
Type of contents on CQICH for this MS \\
0b000 = Default feedback \\
0b001 = Precoding weight matrix W \\
0b010= Channel matrix H \\
0b011 = MIMO mode and permutation zone \\
0b100-0b111 = Reserved
\end{tabular} \\
\hline CQICH_Num & 2 bits & Total number of CQICHs assigned to this MS is (CQICH_Num +1) \\
\hline for ( $\mathbf{i}=\mathbf{1 ; i}$ <CQICH_Num;i++) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Allocation index & 6 bits & Index to uniquely identify the additional CQICH resources assigned to the MS \\
\hline \} & & \\
\hline Padding & variable & The padding bits are used to ensure the contents within each layer loop are an integer number of bytes. Shall be set to zero \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\section*{Insert new subclause 6.3.2.3.43.6.8:}

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.6.8 HARQ Compact_DL-MAP IE format for Switch HARQ Mode}

In the HARQ-MAP, a BS may transmit DL-MAP Type $=7$ with the Switch HARQ mode IE. Allocations subsequent to this IE shall be for the HARQ mode identified.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 101d-HARQ Compact_DL-MAP IE format for switch HARQ mode}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline Compact_DL-MAP_IE () \{ & - & - \\
\hline DL-MAP Type = 7 & 3 bits & - \\
\hline DL-MAP subtype & 5 bits & Extension subtype Value = 1 \\
\hline Length & 4 bits & Length of the IE in Bytes \\
\hline HARQ mode & 4 bits & Subtype dependent payload \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\section*{DL-MAP Type}

The DL-MAP Type value specifies the type of the Ceompact_DL-MAP IE. A value of 7 indicates the extension type.

\section*{DL-MAP Subtype}

The DL-MAP Subtype value specifies the extended map type defined in Table 101a as HARQ mode switch.

\section*{Length}

This indicates the length of this IE in Bytes. This is encoded as 2.

\section*{HARQ mode}

The HARQ mode is a 4-bit value that specifies the HARQ mode for all subsequent Compact_DLMAP IEs to the end of the current HARQ map. See 8.4.9.5 for encoding of this value.

\section*{Insert new subclause 6.3.2.3.43.6.9:}

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.6.9 HARQ Compact MBS MAP IE}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 101e-HARQ Compact MBS_MAP_IE format for extension}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline Compact MBS_MAP_IE\{ & - & - \\
\hline DL_MAP Type = 3 & 3 bits & - \\
\hline Multicast CID & 12 bits & 12 LSBs of CID for multicast \\
\hline MBS Zone Identifier & 7 bits & - \\
\hline Macro diversity enhanced & 1 bit & - \\
\hline If (macro diversity enhanced $=1$ )\{ & - & - \\
\hline Permutation & 2 bits & - \\
\hline Idcell & 6 bits & - \\
\hline OFDMA Symbol Offset & 8 bits & OFDMA symbol offset with respect to start of the MBS portion \\
\hline N_EP code & 4 bits & The combination of $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{EP}}$ code and $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{SCH}}$ code indicates the number of allocated subchannels and scheme of coding and modulation for the MBS_MAP message in MBS portion \\
\hline N_SCH code & 4 bits & - \\
\hline \} else \{ & - & - \\
\hline N_EP & 4 bits & The combination of $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{EP}}$ code and $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{SCH}}$ code indicates the number of allocated subchannels and scheme of coding and modulation for the DL burst \\
\hline N_SCH code & 4 bits & - \\
\hline AI_SN & 1 bits & - \\
\hline SPID & 2 bits & - \\
\hline ACID & 4 bits & - \\
\hline Next MBS frame offset & 8 bits & The Next MBS frame offset value is lower 8 bits of the frame number in which the BS shall transmit the next MBS frame \\
\hline Next MBS OFDMA Symbol offset & 8 bits & The offset of the OFDMA symbol in which the next MBS portion starts, measured in OFDMA symbols from the beginning of the downlink frame in which the MBS_MAP is transmitted \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline if !(byte boundary) \{ & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 101e-HARQ Compact MBS_MAP_IE format for extension (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline Padding Nibble & variable & Padding to reach byte boundary \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\section*{AI SN}

Defines ARQ Identifier Sequence Number. This is toggled between 0 and 1 on successfully transmitting each encoder packet with the same ARQ channel.

\section*{SPID}

Defines SubPacket ID, which is used to identify the four subpackets generated from an encoder packet.

\section*{ACID}

Defines ARQ Channel ID for TimeDiversity MBS packet. Each TimeDiversity MBS connection can have multiple ARQ channels, each of which may have an encoder packet transaction pending.
The MBS burst indicated by the HARQ Compact MBS_MAP_IE is encoded at the same way of HARQ. But it does not need the acknowledgement from MS.

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.7 UL-MAP_IE}

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.7.1 Compact UL-MAP IE for normal subchannel}

\section*{Change the first paragraph as indicated:}

The format of Compact UL-MAP IE for normal subchannel is presented in Table 102. The direction of slot allocation and the direction of data mapping for uplink shall be according to 8.4.3.4.

\section*{Change Table 102 and the text that follows as indicated:}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 102-HARQ HARQ Compact_UL-MAP IE format for normal subchannel}

\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline Compact_UL-MAP_IE () \{ & - & - \\
\hline UL-MAP Type =0 & 3 bits & - \\
\hline Reserved & 1 bit & Shall be set to zero \\
\hline RCID_IE & variable & - \\
\hline if (HARQ mode $=0$ ) \{ & - & CTC IR \\
\hline $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{EP}}$ code & 4 bits & Code of encoder packet bits (see 8.4.9.2.3.5) \\
\hline $\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{S C H}}$ code & 4 bits & Code of allocated subchannels (see 8.4.9.2.3.5) \\
\hline else if (HARQ mode = 1) \{ & - & Generic Chase \\
\hline Shortened UIUC & 4 bits & Shortened UIUC \\

\end{tabular}

\caption{Table 102-H-ARQ HARQ Compact_UL-MAP IE format for normal subchannel (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline I & - & - \\
\hline HARQHARQ_Control_IE & variable & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\

\end{tabular}

\section*{UL-MAP Type}

This The UL-MAP Type value specifies the type of the compact UL-MAP IE. A value of 0 indicates the Normal Subchannel.

\section*{RCID_IE}

Represent the assignment of the IE.
$\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{E P}}$ code, $\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{S C H}}$ code
The combination of $N_{E P}$ code and $N_{S C H}$ code indicates the number of allocated subchannels and scheme of coding and modulation for the UL burst.

\section*{Shortened UIUC}

A shortened version of the UIUC. The shortened UIUC takes on values $1 . .8$ of the UIUC as defined in the UCD. See 8.4.5.4.1.

\section*{Companded SC}

The Companded SC indicates the number of allocated subchannels.

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.43.7.2 Compact UL-MAP IE for Band AMC Subchannel}

\section*{Change the first paragraph as indicated:}

The format of Compact UL-MAP IE for Band AMC Subchannel is presented in Table 103. Slots for uplink AMC zone are allocated along the symbol index first within a band. The direction of data mapping for uplink AMC zone slots shall be frequency first (across bands when multiple bands are allocated).

\section*{Change Table 103 and the text that follows as indicated:}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 103-H-ARQ HARQ Compact_UL-MAP IE format for band AMC}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline Compact_UL-MAP_IE () \{ & - & - \\
\hline UL-MAP Type =band & 3 bits & - \\
\hline Reserved & 1 bit & Shall be set to zero \\
\hline RCID_IE & variable & - \\
\hline if (HARQ mode $=0)\{$ & - & CTC IR \\
\hline $\boldsymbol{N}_{\boldsymbol{E} \boldsymbol{P}}$ code & 4 bits & Code of encoder packet bits (see 8.4.9.2.3.5) \\
\hline $\boldsymbol{N}_{\boldsymbol{S} \boldsymbol{C} \boldsymbol{H}} \boldsymbol{c o d e}$ & 4 bits & Code of allocated subchannels (see 8.4.9.2.3.5) \\
\hline else if (HARQ mode = 1) \{ & - & Generic Chase \\
\hline Shortened UIUC & 3 bits & Shortened UIUC \\
\hline Companded SC & 5 bits & Code of allocated subchannels (see 8.4.9.5) \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 103-H-ARQ HARQ Compact_UL-MAP IE format for band AMC (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline 1 & - & - \\
\hline Nband & Nb-Band & Indicates the number of selected bands. $0=$ BITMAP indicates the number and offset of selected bands \\
\hline if ( Nband $==0$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Band BITMAP & NbBITMAP & $n$-th LSB is 1 if $n$-th band is selected \\
\hline \} else \{ & - & - \\
\hline for ( $\mathrm{i}=0$; < Nband ; $\mathrm{i}++$ ) & - & - \\
\hline Band Index & Nb-Index & Band selection. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline Allocation Mode & 2 bits & Indicates the subchannel allocation mode. 0b00 = same number of subchannels for the selected bands 0b01 = different number of subchannels for the selected bands 0b10 = total number of subchannels for the selected bands determined by $N_{S C H}$ code 0b11 = Reserved \\
\hline Reserved & 2 bits & Shall be set to zero \\
\hline if (Allocation Mode == 0b00)\{ & - & - \\
\hline No. Subchannels & 8 bits & - \\
\hline \} else if (Allocation Mode == 1) \{ & - & - \\
\hline for $(\mathrm{i}=0 ; \mathrm{i}<$ band count; $\mathrm{i}++)$ & - & If Nband is 0 , band count is the number of 1 in Band BITMAP. Otherwise band count is Nband. \\
\hline No. Subchannels & 8 bits & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline H-ARQHARQ_Control_IE & variable & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\section*{UL-MAP Type}

This The UL-MAP Type value specifies the type of the compact UL-MAP IE. A value of 1 indicates the Band AMC Subchannel.

\section*{RCID_IE}

Represent the assignment of the IE.

\section*{$\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{E P}}$ code, $\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{S C H}}$ code}

The combination of $N_{E P}$ code and $N_{S C H}$ code indicates the number of allocated subchannels and scheme of coding and modulation for the UL burst.

\section*{Shortened UIUC}

A shortened version of the UIUC. The shortened UIUC takes on values $1 . .8$ of the UIUC as defined in the UCD. See 8.4.5.4.1.

\section*{Companded SC}

The Companded SC indicates the number of allocated subchannels.

\section*{Nband}

Indicates the number of bands selected for the burst. If this value is set to 0 , the Band BITMAP is used to indicate the number and the position of selected bands instead. The number of the maximum logical bands determines the length of this field.

\section*{Band BITMAP}

This The Band BITMAP is valid when Nband is 0 . The $n$-th LSB of the Band BITMAP is set to 1 when the $n$-th logical band is selected for the burst. If the number of the maximum logical bands is 12 then the length of the Band BITMAP is 12 bits. The band count is set to the number of 1 s in the Band BITMAP. The number of the maximum logical bands determines the length of this field.

\section*{Band Index}

This The Band Index value indexes the selected band offset and is valid when Nband is larger than 0 . The number of the maximum logical bands determines the length of this field.

\section*{Allocation Mode}

This The Allocation Mode value indicates the subchannel allocation mode in the selected bands. The value is set to 0 b 00 when the same numbers of subchannels are allocated in the selected bands by the following field 'No. Subchannels'. The value is set to 0 b 01 when different numbers of subchannels are allocated in each selected bands by the following fields "No. Subchannels". The value is set to $\underline{0 \mathrm{~b}} 10$ when the total number of subchannels allocated in the selected bands is defined by $N_{S C H}$ code and $N_{E P}$ code. The subchannels fill from the bands with lowest index. The allocation mode variant is shown in Figure 23.

\section*{No. Subchannels}

This The No. Subchannels value indicates the number of subchannels allocated for this burst.

\section*{UL-MAP Type}

This The UL-MAP Type value specifies the type of the compact UL-MAP IE. A value of 2 indicates the Safety Subchannel.

\section*{RCID_IE}

Represent the assignment of the IE.
$\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{E P}}$ code, $\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{S C H}}$ code
The combination of $N_{E P}$ code and $N_{S C H}$ code indicates the number of allocated subchannels and scheme of coding and modulation for the UL burst.

\section*{Shortened UIUC}

A shortened version of the UIUC. The shortened UIUC takes on values $1 . .8$ of the UIUC as defined in the UCD. See 8.4.5.4.1.

\section*{Companded SC}

The Companded SC indicates the number of allocated subchannels.

\section*{BIN Offset}

The offset of the BIN allocated for this UL burst.
