\hline TEMP_BS_ID & 3 bits & Diversity Set member ID assigned to this BS. \\
\hline for ( $\mathbf{j}=\mathbf{0} ; \mathbf{j}<$ N_CIDs; $\mathbf{j}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline New CID for BS_i & 16 bits & New CID to be used for new BS_i. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline for $(\mathrm{i}=0 ; \mathrm{i}<$ N_SAIDs; $\mathrm{i}++)$ \{ & & \\
\hline New SAID for BS_i & 16 bits & New SAID to be used for new BS_i. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline CQICH_ID & variable & Index to uniquely identify he CQICH resource assigned to the MS after the MS switched to the new anchor BS . \\
\hline Feedback channel offset & 6 bits & Index to the fast-feedback channel region of the new Anchor BS marked by UIUC. \\
\hline Period (p) & 2 bits & A CQI feedback is transmitted on the CQICH every $2^{\mathrm{p}}$ frames. \\
\hline Frame offset & 3 bits & The MS starts reporting at the frame of which the number has the same 3 LSB as the specified frame offset. If the current frame is specified, the MS should start reporting in eight frames. \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109I-MOB_BSHO-REQ message format (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline Duration (d) & 3 bits & A CQI feedback is transmitted on the CQI channels indexed by the CQICH_ID for $10 \times 2^{d}$ frames. If $d==0 \mathrm{~b} 000$, the CQI-CH is de-allocated. If $d==0 \mathrm{~b} 111$, the MS should report until the BS command for the MS to stop. \\
\hline MIMO_permutation_feedback_cycle & 2 bits & 0b00 = No MIMO and permutation mode feedback 0b01 = the MIMO and permutation mode indication shall be transmitted on the CQICH indexed by the CQICH_ID every 4 CQICH transmission opportunities allocated to the MS in this message. The first indication is sent on the $4{ }^{\text {th }}$ CQICH transmission opportunity allocated to the MS in this message. 0b10 = the MIMO mode and permultation mode indication shall be transmitted on the CQICH indexed by the CQICH_ID every 8 CQICH transmission opportunities allocated to the MS in this message. The first indication is sent on the $8{ }^{\text {th }}$ CQICH transmission opportunity allocated to the MS in this message. 0b11 = the MIMO mode and permultation mode indication shall be transmitted on the CQICH indexed by the CQICH_ID every 16 CQICH transmission opportunities allocated to the MS in this message. The first indication is sent on the $16{ }^{\text {th }}$ CQICH transmission opportunity allocated to the MS in this message. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline N_current_BSs & 3 bits & Number of BSs currently in the Diversity Set of the MS, which are recommended to be remained in the Diversity Set. \\
\hline for ( $\mathrm{i}=0 ; \mathrm{i}<$ N_current_BSs; $\mathrm{i}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline TEMP_BS_ID & 3 bits & Diversity Set member ID assigned to this BS. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline TEMP_BS_ID_Anchor & 3 bits & Temp BSID for Anchor BS. \\
\hline AK Change Indicator & 1 bit & \begin{tabular}{l}
To indicate whether the AK being used should change when switching to a new Anchor BS. If set to 0 , the MS should continue to use the AK currently in use. \\
If set to 1 , the MS should use the AK derived for use with the new Anchor BS.
\end{tabular} \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline Action time & 7 bits & - \\
\hline padding & variable & Padding bits to ensure byte aligned. \\
\hline TLV encoded information & variable & TLV specific. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

A BS shall generate MOB_BSHO-REQ messages in the format shown in Table 1091. The following parameters shall be included in the MOB_BSHO-REQ message:

\section*{Mode}

Indicates which HO mode is for this handover request.
0b000: HO request
0b001: MDHO/FBSS request: Anchor BS update with CID update
0b010: MDHO/FBSS request: Anchor BS update without CID update
0b011: MDHO/FBSS request: Diversity Set update with CID update
0b100: MDHO/FBSS request: Diversity Set update without CID update
0b101: MDHO/FBSS request: Diversity Set update with CID update for newly added BS
0b110: MDHO/FBSS request: Diversity Set update with CID update and CQICH allocation for newly added BS
0b111: Reserved

\section*{HO operation mode}

Indicate the operation mode of this HO request as initiated and prescribed by BS .
0: Recommended HO request
1: Mandatory HO request. If HO operation mode is set to 1, BS shall include at least one recommended BS in the message ( N_Recommended $>=1$ ).
Resource Retain Flag
The Resource Retain Flag indicates whether the serving BS will retain or delete the connection information of the MS upon receiving MOB_HO-IND with HO_IND_type $=0 \mathrm{~b} 00$. If the flag is set to 1 , the serving BS will retain the MS's connection information during the time in Resource Retain Time field. If Resource Retain Flag $=1$ and Resource Retain Time is not included as a TLV item in the message, then the serving BS and MS shall use the System Resource Retain Time timer. If the flag is set to 0 , the serving BS will discard the MS's connection information.
Action Time
For HO, this value is defined as number of frames until the Target BS allocates a dedicated transmission opportunity for RNG-REQ message to be transmitted by the MS using Fast_Ranging_IE. Dedicated allocation for transmission of RNG-REQ means that channel parameters learned by the MS during Association of that BS stay valid and can be reused during actual Network Re-entry without preceding CDMA-based Initial Ranging. This parameter is decided by the Serving BS based on the information obtained from potential Target BSs over the backbone.
For MDHO/FBSS, this is the time of update of Anchor BS and/or Diversity Set. A value of zero in this parameter signifies that this parameter should be ignored.

For Mode $=0 \mathrm{~b} 00$, for each recommended neighbor BS, the following parameters shall be included:

\section*{Network Assisted HO supported}

Indicates that the BS supports Network Assisted HO, 1=supported, 0=not supported
Neighbor BSID
Same as the Base Station ID parameter in the DL-MAP message of neighbor BS. This may include the serving BS.

\section*{Preamble index/ Subchannel Index}

For the SCa and OFDMA PHY this parameter defines the PHY specific preamble for the neighbor BS. For the OFDM PHY the 5 LSB contain the DL subchannel index (as defined in Table 211) used in the Neighbor BS' sector. The 3 MSB shall be Reserved and set to 0 b 000 .

\section*{Service level prediction}

The service level prediction value indicates the level of service the MS can expect from this BS. The following encodings apply:
$0=$ No service possible for this MS
1 = Some service is available for one or several service flows authorized for the MS.

2 = For each authorized service flow, a MAC connection can be established with QoS specified by the AuthorizedQoSParamSet.
3 = No service level prediction available.

\section*{HO process optimization}

HO Process Optimization is provided as part of this message is indicative only. HO process requirements may change at time of actual HO . For each Bit location, a value of ' 0 ' indicates the associated reentry management messages shall be required, a value of ' 1 ' indicates the reentry management message may be omitted. Regardless of the HO Process Optimization TLV settings, the target BS may send unsolicited SBC-RSP and/ or REG-RSP management messages:

Bit \#0: Omit SBC-REQ/RSP management messages during re-entry processing
Bit \#1: Omit PKM Authentication phase except TEK phase during current re-entry processing
Bit \#2: Omit PKM TEK creation phase during re-entry processing
Bit \#3: Omit REG-REQ/RSP management during current re-entry processing
Bit \#4: Omit Network Address Acquisition management messages during current re-entry processing
Bit \#5: Omit Time of Day Acquisition management messages during current reentry processing
Bit \#6: Omit TFTP management messages during current re-entry processing
Bit \#7: Full service and operational state transfer or sharing between serving BS and target BS (ARQ, timers, counters, MAC state machines, etc...)

For Mode $!=0 \mathrm{~b} 000$, the following parameters shall be included:

\section*{N_new_BSs}

Number of new BSs that are recommended to be added to the Diversity Set of the MS.

\section*{N_CID}

Number of CIDs that need to be allocated.

\section*{TEMP BSID}

Index to diversity set for active BS ranging from 0 to 7.

\section*{New CID}

The New CIDs shall be set according to the followings: the first CID in the list shall be basic CID; the second CID in the list shall be primary management CID, the third CID in the list shall be Secondary Management CID if secondary management connection is established for the MS at the current serving BS. The remaining CIDs shall be transport CIDs, multicast CIDs are enumerated by the ascending order of corresponding current SFIDs. The MS shall store the CIDs associated with the newly added BS and using the CIDs when the newly added BS becomes the anchor BS.

\section*{N_SAIDs}

Number of SAIDs that need to be assigned.

\section*{New SAID}

New SAIDs are enumerated by the ascending order of corresponding current SAIDs. The MS shall store the SAIDs associated with the newly added BS and using the SAIDs when the newly added BS becomes the anchor BS.

\section*{HO_ID included indicator}

Indicates whether HO_ID is included or not in this message.

\section*{HO_ID}

ID assigned for use in initial ranging to the target BS once this BS is selected as the target BS (see 6.3.20.5).

\section*{AK Change Indicator}

Indicates whether the AK being used should change when switching to a new Anchor BS . If set to 0 , the MS should continue to use the AK currently in use; if set to 1 , the MS should use the AK derived for use with the new Anchor BS.

The MOB_BSHO-REQ may contain the following TLV:

\section*{Resource Retain Time (see 11.15.1)}

The MOB_BSHO-REQ message shall include the following parameter encoded as TLV tuples:

\section*{HMAC/CMAC Tuple (see 11.1.2)}

The HMAC/CMAC Tuple shall be the last attribute in the message.

\section*{Insert new subclause 6.3.2.3.53:}

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.53 MS HO Request (MOB_MSHO-REQ) message}

The MS may transmit an MOB_MSHO-REQ message when it wants to initiate an HO. The message shall be transmitted on the basic CID (Table 109m).

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109m-MOB_MSHO-REQ message format}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline MOB_MSHO-REQ_Message_format() \{ & - & - \\
\hline Management Message Type = 57 & 8 bits & - \\
\hline Report metric & 8 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
Bitmap indicating presence of metric in message \\
Bit \#0: BS CINR mean \\
Bit \#1: BS RSSI mean \\
Bit \#2: Relative delay \\
Bit \#3: BS RTD; this metric shall be only measured on serving BS/anchor BS. Bits \#4-7: Reserved; shall be set to zero.
\end{tabular} \\
\hline N_New_BS_Index & 8 bits & Number of new recommended BSs which are included in MOB_NBR-ADV message. \\
\hline If(N_New_BS_Index!=0)\{ & & \\
\hline Configuration change count for MOB_NBRADV & 8 bits & Configuration Change Count value of referring MOB_NBR-ADV message. \\
\hline \} & & \\
\hline For(j=0;j< N_New_BS_Index;j++)\{ & & \\
\hline Neighbor_BS_Index & 8 bits & \\
\hline Preamble index/ Preamble Present and Subchannel Index & 8 bits & For the SCa and OFDMA PHY this parameter defines the PHY specific preamble for the neighbor BS . For the OFDM PHY the 5 LSB contain the active DL subchannel index for the neighbor BS. The 3 MSB shall be Reserved and set to '0b000'. \\
\hline If(Report metric[Bit\#0]==1) & & \\
\hline BS CINR mean & 8 bits & \\
\hline If(Report metric[Bit\#1]==1) & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109m-MOB_MSHO-REQ message format (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline BS RSSI mean & 8 bits & \\
\hline If(Report metric[Bit\#2]==1) & & \\
\hline Relative delay & 8 bits & \\
\hline Service level prediction & 3 bits & \\
\hline Arrival Time Difference Indication & 1 bit & If the MS is transmitting this message to request HO or MDHO/FBSS is not supported by either BS or MS, this bit shall be set to 0 . \\
\hline If (Arrival Time Difference Indication == 1) \{ & & \\
\hline Arrival Time Difference (t) & 4 bits & Relative difference in arrival time between the neighbor BS and the anchor BS , in terms of fraction of CP. \\
\hline \} & & \\
\hline \} & & \\
\hline N_New_BS_Full & & \\
\hline For(j=0;j< N_New_BS_Full;j++)\{ & & \\
\hline Neighbor_BS_ID & 8 bits & \\
\hline Preamble index/ Preamble Present and Subchannel Index & 8 bits & For the SCa and OFDMA PHY this parameter defines the PHY specific preamble for the neighbor BS . For the OFDM PHY the 5 LSB contain the active DL subchannel index for the neighbor BS. The 3 MSB shall be Reserved and set to '0b000'. \\
\hline If (Report metric [Bit\#0] == 1) & - & - \\
\hline BS CINR mean & 8 bits & - \\
\hline If(Report metric[Bit\#1]==1) & - & - \\
\hline BS RSSI mean & 8 bits & - \\
\hline If(Report metric[Bit\#2]==1) & - & - \\
\hline Relative delay & 8 bits & - \\
\hline Service level prediction & 3 bits & - \\
\hline Arrival Time Difference Indication & 1 bit & If the MS is transmitting this message to request HO or MDHO/FBSS is not supported by either BS or MS, this bit shall be set to 0 . \\
\hline If (Arrival Time Difference Indication == 1) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Arrival Time Difference (t) & 4 bits & Relative difference in arrival time between the neighbor BS and the anchor BS , in terms of fraction of CP. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109m-MOB_MSHO-REQ message format (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline N_current_BSs & 3 bits & When FBSS/MDHO is supported and the MS has non-empty diversity set, N_current_BSs is the number of BSs that are currently in the Diversity Set of the MS When FBSS/MDHO is not supported or the MS has an empty diversity set, N_current_BSs is set to 1 . \\
\hline Padding & 1 bit & Shall be set to zero. \\
\hline For ( $\mathrm{j}=0 ; \mathrm{j}<$ N_current_BSs ; $\mathrm{j}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Temp BSID & 4 bits & Diversity Set member ID assigned to this BS. When the MS has an empty diversity set or FBSS/MDHO is not supported, Temp BSID shall be set to 0 . \\
\hline If(Report metric[Bit\#0]==1) & - & - \\
\hline BS CINR mean & 8 bits & - \\
\hline If(Report metric[Bit\#1]==1) & - & - \\
\hline BS RSSI mean & 8 bits & - \\
\hline If(Report metric[Bit\#2]==1) & - & - \\
\hline Relative delay & 8 bits & Only when FBSS/MDHO is in progress, this field will include the relative delay of BSs currently in the diversity set, except anchor BS . \\
\hline If(Report metric[Bit\#3]==1) & - & - \\
\hline BS RTD & 8 bits & This field will include the RTD of the serving $\mathrm{BS} /$ anchor BS . \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline Padding & variable & Padding bits to ensure byte aligned. \\
\hline TLV encoded information & variable & \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

An MS shall generate MOB_MSHO-REQ messages in the format shown in Table 109m. The following parameters shall be included in the MOB_MSHO-REQ message:

\section*{Report metric}

Bitmap indicator of trigger metrics that the MS reports in this message. MS shall indicate only the trigger metrics agreed during SBC-REQ/RSP negotiation For each bit location, a value of ' 0 ' indicates the trigger metric should not be included, while a value of ' 1 ' indicates the trigger metric should be included in the message. The bitmap interpretation for the metrics shall be:

Bit 0: BS CINR mean
Bit 1: BS RSSI mean
Bit 2: Relative delay
Bit 3: BS RTD; this metric shall be only measured on serving BS/anchor BS
Bits 4-7: Reserved; shall be set to zero

\section*{N_New_BS_Index}

Number of neighboring BSs to be considered for handover, which are included in MOB_NBRADV message. The number could be larger than zero only when NBR_BS_Index_Validity_Time is larger than the difference of MOB_MSHO-REQ message transmitting time and MOB_NBR-ADV message receiving time (MOB_NBR-ADV message should be referred).

\section*{N_New_BS_Full}

Number of neighboring BSs to be scanned or associated, which are not included in MOB_NBR-ADV message.

For each recommended neighbor BS, the following parameters shall be included,

\section*{Configuration Change Count for MOB_NBR-ADV}

The value of Configuration Change Count in MOB_NBR-ADV message referred in order to compress neighbor BSID.

\section*{Neighbor_BS_Index}

BS index corresponds to position of BS in MOB_NBR-ADV message.

\section*{Neighbor BSID}

Same as the Base Station ID parameter in the DL-MAP message of the Neighbor BS.

\section*{Preamble index/ Subchannel Index}

For the SCa and OFDMA PHY this parameter defines the PHY specific preamble for the neighbor BS. For the OFDM PHY the 5 LSB contain the DL subchannel index (as defined in Table 211) used in the Neighbor BS' sector. The 3 MSB shall be Reserved and set to 0 .

According to Report metric that MS indicates, the MOB_MSHO-REQ message includes the following parameters:

\section*{BS CINR mean}

The BS CINR mean parameter indicates the CINR in dB measured at the MS on the downlink signal of a particular BS. The value shall be interpreted as a signed byte with the resolution of 0.5 dB . The measurement shall be performed on the subcarriers of the frame preamble that are active in the particular BS's segment and averaged over the measurement period.

\section*{BS RSSI mean}

The BS RSSI mean parameter indicates the Received Signal Strength measured by the MS from the particular BS. The value shall be interpreted as an unsigned byte with units of 0.25 dB , such that $0 \times 00$ is interpreted as -103.75 dBm , an MS shall be able to report values in the range -103.75 dBm to -40 dBm . The measurement shall be performed on the frame preamble and averaged over the measurement period.

\section*{Relative delay}

This parameter indicates the delay of neighbor DL signals relative to the serving BS, as measured by the MS for the particular BS. The value shall be interpreted as a signed integer in units of samples.

\section*{BS RTD}

The BS RTD parameter indicates the round trip delay (RTD) measured by the MS from the serving BS. RTD can be RTD can be given by the latest time advance taken by MS. The value shall be interpreted as an unsigned byte with units of $1 / \mathrm{Fs}$ (see 10.3.4.3). This parameter shall be only measured on serving BS/anchor BS.

\section*{Service level prediction}

The service level prediction value indicates the level of service the MS can expect from this BS. The following encodings apply:
0 = No service possible for this MS
1 = Some service is available for one or several service flows authorized for the MS
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2=\text { For each authorized service flow, a MAC connection can be established with QoS specified } \\
& \text { by the AuthorizedQoSParamSet. } \\
& 3=\text { No service level prediction available. }
\end{aligned}
$$

\section*{Arrival Time Difference}

The Arrival Time Difference parameter indicates the delay of downlink signal relative to the serving BS, as measured by the MS for the neighbor BS. For SCa PHY mode, this value shall be interpreted as a signed byte with the resolution of PS. For OFDM and OFDMA PHY mode, this value shall be interpreted as a signed fraction with a range of $+7 / 8$ to -1 one cyclic prefix time of the serving BS. A positive value indicates that the signal of the neighbor BS arrived after that of the serving BS (for example, the value of 0x02 indicates that the neighbor signal is delayed by $25 \% \pm 6.25 \%$ of the CP).

When the MS supports FBSS/MDHO and has a non-empty diversity set, the MS shall include the following parameters for each active BS. When the MS does not support FBSS/MDHO or has an empty active, the MS shall include the following parameters for the current serving BS.

\section*{Temp BSID}

When the MS support FBSS/MDHO and has a non-empty diversity set, Temp BSID is the diversity set member ID. When the MS does not support FBSS/MDHO or has an empty diversity set, Temp BSID shall be set to 0 .

\section*{BS CINR mean}

The BS CINR mean parameter indicates the CINR in dB measured at the MS on the downlink signal of a particular BS. The value shall be interpreted as a signed byte with the resolution of 0.5 dB . The measurement shall be performed on the frame preamble and averaged over the measurement period.

The MOB_MSHO-REQ message shall include the following parameters encoded as TLV tuples:

\section*{HMAC/CMAC Tuple (See 11.1.2.)}

The HMAC/CMAC Tuple shall be the last attribute in the message.

\section*{Insert new subclause 6.3.2.3.54:}

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.54 BS HO Response (MOB_BSHO-RSP) message}

The BS shall transmit an MOB_BSHO-RSP message upon reception of MOB_MSHO-REQ message. The message shall be transmitted on the basic CID.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109n-MOB_BSHO-RSP message format}
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline MOB_BSHO-RSP_Message_format () \{ & - & - \\
\hline Management Message Type $=\mathbf{5 8}$ & 8 bits & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109n-MOB_BSHO-RSP message format (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline Mode & 3 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
0b000: HO request \\
0b001: MDHO/FBSS request: Anchor BS update with CID update \\
0b010: MDHO/FBSS request: Anchor BS update without CID update \\
0b011: MDHO/FBSS request: Diversity Set update with CID update \\
0b100: MDHO/FBSS request: Diversity Set update without CID update \\
0b101: MDHO/FBSS request: Diversity Set update with CID update for newly added BS \\
0b110: MDHO/FBSS request: Diversity Set update with CID update and CQICH allocation for newly added BS \\
0b111: MS handover request not recommended (BS in list unavailable)
\end{tabular} \\
\hline Reserved & 5 bits & Shall be set to zero. \\
\hline If (Mode == 0b000) \{ & - & - \\
\hline HO operation mode & 1 bit & \begin{tabular}{l}
0: Recommended HO response. \\
1: Mandatory HO response.
\end{tabular} \\
\hline N_Recommended & 8 bits & - \\
\hline Resource Retain Flag & 1 bit & \begin{tabular}{l}
0: Release connection information. \\
1: Retain connection information.
\end{tabular} \\
\hline Reserved & 7 bits & Shall be set to zero. \\
\hline For ( $\mathrm{j}=0$; j $<$ N_Recommended ; j++) \{ & - & Neighbor base stations shall be presented in an order such that the first presented is the one most recommended and the last presented is the least recommended. \\
\hline Neighbor BSID & 48 bits & - \\
\hline Preamble index/ Preamble Present and Subchannel Index & 8 bits & For the SCa and OFDMA PHY this parameter defines the PHY specific preamble for the neighbor BS. For the OFDM PHY the 5 LSB contain the active DL subchannel index for the neighbor BS. The 3 MSB shall be Reserved and set to '0b000'. \\
\hline Service level prediction & 8 bits & - \\
\hline HO process optimization & 8 bits & - \\
\hline Network Assisted HO supported & 1 bit & Indicates that the BS supports Network Assisted HO. \\
\hline HO_ID_included_indicator & 1 bit & Indicates if the field HO_IND is included. \\
\hline If (HO_ID_included_indicator == 1) \{ & - & - \\
\hline HO_ID & 8 bits & ID assigned for use in initial ranging to the target BS once this BS is selected as the target BS . \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109n-MOB_BSHO-RSP message format (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline HO_authorization policy indicator & 1 bit & \begin{tabular}{l}
To indicate if authorization negotiation is used in HO procedure. \\
0: EAP authorization and the value of the MAC mode field in the current BS (default) \\
1: The authorization policy for the target BS is negotiated.
\end{tabular} \\
\hline Reserved & 4 bits & Shall be set to zero. \\
\hline If (HO_authorization policy indicator == 1) \{ & - & - \\
\hline HO_authorization_policy_support & 8 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
Bit \#0: RSA authorization \\
Bit \#1: EAP authorization \\
Bit \#2: Authenticated-EAP authorization \\
Bit \#3: HMAC supported \\
Bit \#4: CMAC supported \\
Bit \#5: 64-bit Short-HMAC \\
Bit \#6: 80-bit Short-HMAC \\
Bit \#7: 96-bit Short-HMAC
\end{tabular} \\
\hline \} & & \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline else if (Mode == 0b001) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Temp BSID & 3 bits & TEMP_BSID of the recommended Anchor BS . \\
\hline AK Change Indicator & 1 bit & \begin{tabular}{l}
To indicate whether the AK being used should change when switching to a new Anchor BS. \\
If set to 0 , the MS should continue to use the AK currently in use. \\
If set to 1 , the MS should use the AK derived for use with the new Anchor BS.
\end{tabular} \\
\hline N_CIDs & 8 bits & Number of CIDs that need to be reassigned. For MDHO, N_CIDs shall be set to zero. \\
\hline For ( $\mathrm{i}=0$;i<N_CIDs;i++) \{ & - & - \\
\hline New CID & 16 bits & New CID to be used after Diversity Set is updated. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline N_SAIDs & 8 bits & Number of SAIDs that need to be reassigned. \\
\hline For(i=0; i<N_SAIDs; i++)\{ & & \\
\hline New SAID & 16 bits & New SAID to be used after Anchor BS is updated. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline else if (Mode == 0b010) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Temp BSID & 3 bits & TEMP_BSID of the recommended Anchor BS . \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

If set to 0 , the MS should continue to use the AK currently in use. \\
If set to 1 , the MS should use the AK derived for use with the new Anchor BS.

\hline N_CIDs & 8 bits & Number of CIDs that need to be reassigned. \\
\hline For ( $\mathrm{j}=0 ; \mathrm{j}<$ N_CIDs ; $\mathrm{j}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\

\hline \} & - & - \\

\hline For ( $\mathrm{j}=0 ; \mathrm{j}<\mathrm{N} \_$new_BSs ; $\mathrm{j}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109n-MOB_BSHO-RSP message format (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline Neighbor BSID & 48 bits & - \\
\hline Temp BSID & 3 bits & Diversity Set member ID assigned to this BS. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline N_current_BSs & 3 bits & Number of BSs currently in the Diversity Set of the MS, which are recommended to be remained in the Diversity Set. \\
\hline For ( $\mathrm{j}=0 ; \mathrm{j}<\mathrm{N} \_$current_BSs ; $\mathrm{j}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\

If set to 1 , the MS should use the AK derived for use with the new Anchor BS.
\end{tabular} \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline else if (Mode == 0b101) \{ & - & - \\
\hline N_new_BSs & 3 bits & Number of new BSs that are recommended to be added to the Diversity Set of the MS. \\
\hline N_CIDs & 8 bits & Number of CIDs that need to be reassigned. \\
\hline for ( $\mathrm{i}=0$; $\mathrm{i}<$ N_new_BSs; $\mathrm{i}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Neighbor BSID & 48 bits & - \\
\hline TEMP_BSID & 3 bits & Diversity Set member ID assigned to this BS. \\
\hline for ( $\mathrm{j}=0 ; \mathrm{j}<$ N_CIDs; $\mathrm{j}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline New CID for BS_i & 16 bits & New CID to be used for new BS_i. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline N_current_BSs & 3 bits & Number of BSs currently in the Diversity Set of the MS that are recommended to remain in the Diversity Set. \\
\hline for ( $\mathrm{i}=0 ; \mathrm{i}<$ N_current_BSs; $\mathrm{i}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline TEMP_BSID & 3 bits & Diversity Set member ID assigned to this BS. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline TEMP_BSID_Anchor & 3 bits & Temp BSID for Anchor BS. \\
\hline AK Change Indicator & 1 bit & \begin{tabular}{l}
To indicate whether the AK being used should change when switching to a new Anchor BS. \\
If set to 0 , the MS should continue to use the AK currently in use. \\
If set to 1 , the MS should use the AK derived for use with the new Anchor BS.
\end{tabular} \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109n-MOB_BSHO-RSP message format (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline else if (Mode == 0b110) \{ & - & - \\
\hline N_new_BSs & 3 bits & Number of new BSs that are recommended to be added to the Diversity Set of the MS. \\
\hline N_CIDs & 8 bits & Number of CIDs that need to be reassigned. \\
\hline N_SAIDs & 8 bits & Number of SAIDs need to be reassigned.. \\
\hline for ( $\mathrm{i}=0$; i < N_new_BSs; i ++ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Neighbor BSID & 48 bits & - \\
\hline TEMP_BSID & 3 bits & Diversity Set member ID assigned to this BS. \\
\hline for ( $\mathbf{j}=\mathbf{0} ; \mathbf{j}<$ N_CIDs; $\mathbf{j}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline New CID for BS_i & 16 bits & New CID to be used for new BS_i. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline For $\left(\mathrm{i}=0 ; \mathrm{i}<\mathrm{N} \_\right.$SAIDs; $\left.\mathrm{i}++\right)\{$ & - & - \\
\hline New SAID for BS_i & 16 bits & New SAID to be used for new BS_i. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline CQICH_ID & variable & Index to uniquely identify the CQICH resource assigned to the MS after the MS switched to the new anchor BS. \\
\hline Feedback channel offset & 6 bits & Index to the fast-feedback channel region of the new Anchor BS marked by UIUC. \\
\hline Period (=p) & 2 bits & A CQI feedback is transmitted on the CQICH every $2^{\mathrm{p}}$ frames. \\
\hline Frame offset & 3 bits & The MS starts reporting at the frame of which the number has the same 3 LSB as the specified frame offset. If the current frame is specified, the MS should start reporting in eight frames. \\
\hline Duration (=d) & 3 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
A CQI feedback is transmitted on the CQI channels indexed by the CQICH_ID for $10 \times 2^{\mathrm{d}}$ frames. \\
If $\mathrm{d}==0 \mathrm{~b} 000$, the CQI-CH is de-allocated. If $\mathrm{d}==0 \mathrm{~b} 111$, the MS should report until the BS command for the MS to stop.
\end{tabular} \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109n-MOB_BSHO-RSP message format (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline MIMO_permutation_feedback_cycle & 2 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
0b00= No MIMO and permutation mode feedback \\
0b01 = the MIMO and permutation mode indication shall be transmitted on the CQICH indexed by the CQICH_ID every four CQICH transmission opportunities allocated to the MS in this message. The first indication is sent on the $4{ }^{\text {th }}$ CQICH transmission opportunity allocated to the MS in this message. \\
0b10 = the MIMO mode and permultation mode indication shall be transmitted on the CQICH indexed by the CQICH_ID every eight CQICH transmission opportunities allocated to the MS in this message. The first indication is sent on the $8^{\text {th }}$ CQICH transmission opportunity allocated to the MS in this message. \\
0b11 = the MIMO mode and permultation mode indication shall be transmitted on the CQICH indexed by the CQICH_ID every 16 CQICH transmission opportunities allocated to the MS in this message. The first indication is sent on the $16{ }^{\text {th }}$ CQICH transmission opportunity allocated to the MS in this message.
\end{tabular} \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline N_current_BSs & 3 bits & Number of BSs currently in the Diversity Set of the MS that are recommended to remain in the Diversity Set. \\
\hline for ( $\mathrm{i}=0 ; \mathrm{i}<\mathrm{N} \_$current_BSs; $\mathrm{i}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline TEMP_BSID & 3 bits & Diversity Set member ID assigned to this BS. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline TEMP_BSID_Anchor & 3 bits & Temp BSID for Anchor BS. \\
\hline AK Change Indicator & 1 bit & \begin{tabular}{l}
To indicate whether the AK being used should change when switching to a new Anchor BS. \\
If set to 0 , the MS should continue to use the AK currently in use. \\
If set to 1 , the MS should use the AK derived for use with the new Anchor BS.
\end{tabular} \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline Action time & 8 bits & - \\
\hline padding & variable & Padding bits to ensure byte aligned. \\
\hline TLV encoded information & 2 bits & TLV specific See 11.16.1. \\
\hline \} & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

A BS shall generate MOB_BSHO-RSP messages in the format shown in Table 109n. The following parameters shall be included in the MOB_BSHO-RSP message:

\section*{HO operation mode}

Indicate the operation mode of this HO response as prescribed by BS .
0 : Recommended HO response.
1: Mandatory HO response. If HO operation mode is set to $1, \mathrm{BS}$ shall include at least one recommended BS in the message (N_Recommended >=1).

\section*{Action Time}

For HO , this value is defined as number of frames until the Target BS allocates a dedicated transmission opportunity for RNG-REQ message to be transmitted by the MS using Fast Ranging IE. Non-zero value of this parameter means that potential Target BS estimates that channel parameters learned by the MS during Association of that BS stay valid and can be reused during actual Network Re-entry without preceding CDMA-based Initial Ranging. This parameter is decided by the Serving BS based on the information obtained from potential Target BSs over the backbone.
For MDHO/FBSS, this is the time of update of Anchor BS and/or Diversity Set. A value of zero in this parameter signifies that this parameter should be ignored.
For MS handover request not recommended (Mode $==0 \mathrm{~b} 111$ ), Action Time is the number of frames that the BS suggests MS wait before transmitting a next MOB_MSHO-REQ or MOB_HO-IND. If the action timer is equal to 0 , MS may transmit a revised MOB_MSHOREQ or MOB_HO-IND immediately.

\section*{Resource Retain Flag}

The Resource Retain Flag indicates whether the serving BS will retain or delete the connection information of the MS upon receiving MOB_HO-IND with HO_IND_type $=0 \mathrm{~b} 00$. If the flag is set to 1 , the serving BS will retain the MS's connection information during the time in Resource Retain Time field. If Resource Retain Flag=01 then the serving BS and MS shall use the System Resource Retain Time timer. If the flag is set to 0 , the serving BS will discard the MS's connection information.

For Mode=0b000 for each recommended neighbor BS, the following parameters shall be included,

\section*{NeighborBSID}

Same as the Base Station ID parameter in the DL-MAP message of neighbor BS. This may include the serving BS.

\section*{Preamble index/ Subchannel Index}

For the SCa and OFDMA PHY this parameter defines the PHY specific preamble for the neighbor BS. For the OFDM PHY the 5 LSB contain the DL subchannel index (as defined in Table 211) used in the Neighbor BS' sector. The 3 MSB shall be Reserved and set to 0 .

\section*{Service level prediction}

The service level prediction value indicates the level of service the MS can expect from this BS. The following encodings apply:
$0=$ No service possible for this MS
1 = Some service is available for one or several service flows authorized for the MS.
2 = For each authorized service flow, a MAC connection can be established with QoS specified by the AuthorizedQoSParamSet.
3 = No service level prediction available.

\section*{HO process optimization}

HO Process Optimization is provided as part of this message is indicative only. HO process requirements may change at time of actual HO. For each Bit location, a value of ' 0 ' indicates the associated reentry management messages shall be required, a value of ' 1 ' indicates the reentry management message may be omitted. Regardless of the HO Process Optimization TLV settings, the target BS may send unsolicited SBC-RSP and/ or REG-RSP management messages:

Bit \#0: Omit SBC-REQ/RSP management messages during re-entry processing

Bit \#1: Omit PKM Authentication phase except TEK phase during current re-entry processing
Bit \#2: Omit PKM TEK creation phase during re-entry processing
Bit \#3: Omit REG-REQ/RSP management during current re-entry processing
Bit \#4: Omit Network Address Acquisition management messages during current reentry processing
Bit \#5: Omit Time of Day Acquisition management messages during current reentry processing
Bit \#6: Omit TFTP management messages during current re-entry processing
Bit \#7: Full service and operational state transfer or sharing between serving BS and target BS (ARQ, timers, counters, MAC state machines, etc...)

\section*{HO_ID_included_indicator}

Indicates whether HO_ID is included or not in this message.
The MOB_BSHO-RSP may contain the following TLV:

\section*{Resource Retain Time (See 11.15.1.)}

The MOB_BSHO-RSP message shall include the following parameter encoded as TLV tuples:

\section*{HMAC/CMAC Tuple (See 11.1.2.)}

The HMAC/CMAC Tuple shall be the last attribute in the message.
For Mode $!=0 \mathrm{~b} 000$, the following parameter shall be included:

\section*{AK Change Indicator}

Indicates whether the AK being used should change when switching to a new Anchor BS. If set to 0 , the MS should continue to use the AK currently in use; if set to 1 , the MS should use the AK derived for use with the new Anchor BS.

\section*{Insert new subclause 6.3.2.3.55:}

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.55 HO Indication (MOB_HO-IND) message}

An MS shall transmit a MOB_HO-IND message for final indication that it is about to perform a HO. When the MS cancels or rejects the HO, the MS shall transmit a MOB_HO-IND message with appropriate HO_IND_type field. The message shall be transmitted on the basic CID.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 1090-MOB_HO-IND message format}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline MOB_HO-IND_Message_format() \{ & - & - \\
\hline Management Message Type = 59 & 8 bits & - \\
\hline Reserved & 6 bits & Reserved; shall be set to zero \\
\hline Mode & 2 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
0b00: HO \\
0b01: MDHO/FBSS: Anchor BS update \\
0b10: MDHO/FBSS: Diversity Set update \\
0b11: Reserved
\end{tabular} \\
\hline if (Mode == 0b00) \{ & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 1090-MOB_HO-IND message format (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline HO_IND_type & 2 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
0b00: serving BS release \\
0b01: HO cancel \\
0b10: HO reject \\
0b11: Reserved
\end{tabular} \\
\hline Ranging_Params_valid_indication & 2 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
0b00: No indication. BS ignores this field (Default) \\
0b01: MS ranging parameters for Target BS, which is specified in this message are valid 0b10: MS has no valid ranging parameters for Target BS, which is specified in this message 0b11: Reserved
\end{tabular} \\
\hline Reserved & 4 bits & Shall be set to zero. \\
\hline if (HO_IND_type == 0b00) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Target_BS_ID & 48 bits & Applicable only when HO_IND_type is set to 0b00. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline if (Mode == 0b01) \{ & - & - \\
\hline MDHOFBSS_IND_Type & 2 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
0b00: Confirms Anchor BS update \\
0b01: Anchor BS update cancel \\
0b10: Anchor BS update reject \\
0b11: Reserved
\end{tabular} \\
\hline if (MDHOFBSS_IND_Type == 0b00) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Anchor BSID & 3 bits & TEMP_BSID of the Anchor BS \\
\hline Action time & 8 bits & Action time when the Anchor BS shall be updated. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline if (Mode == 0b10) \{ & - & - \\
\hline MDHOFBSS_IND_Type & 2 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
0b00: Confirms Diversity Set update \\
0b01: Diversity Set update cancel \\
0b10: Diversity set update reject \\
0b11: Reserved
\end{tabular} \\
\hline if (MDHOFBSS_IND_Type == 0b00) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Diversity Set Included Indicator & 1 bit & \begin{tabular}{l}
1: Final decision of Diversity Set members included in the message \\
0: Diversity Set members are as specified in MOB_BSHO_RSP message. No Diversity Set information included in this message.
\end{tabular} \\
\hline \begin{tabular}{l}
if (Diversity Set Included Indicator == \\
1) \{
\end{tabular} & 3 bits & - \\
\hline Anchor BSID & 3 bits & TEMP_BSID of the Anchor BS. \\
\hline N_BSs & 3 bits & Number of BS in the Diversity Set, excluding the Anchor BS. \\
\hline For ( $\mathrm{j}=0 ; \mathrm{j}<\mathrm{N} \_$BSs $; \mathrm{j}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 1090-MOB_HO-IND message format (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Temp BSID & 3 bits & Diversity Set member ID assigned. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & 8 bits & - \\
\hline Action time & 8 bits & Action time when the Anchor BS shall be updated. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline Preamble index/ Subchannel Index & 8 bits & For the SCa and OFDMA PHY this parameter defines the PHY specific preamble for the target BS. For the OFDM PHY the 5 LSB contain the active DL subchannel index for the target BS. The 3 MSB shall be Reserved and set to '0b000'. \\
\hline Padding & variable & Padding bits to ensure byte aligned. Shall be set to zero. \\
\hline TLV encoded information & variable & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

The MS shall use the handover mode signaled by the BS in the previous MOB_BSHO-REQ or MOB_BSHO-RSP message to perform handover.

An MS shall generate MOB_HO-IND messages in the format shown in Table 109o. The following parameters shall be included in the message:

\section*{Ranging Params valid indication}

Indicator that shows whether ranging parameters acquired by the MS during preceding Association with selected Target BS are still valid. This indicator may be used by Target BS in decision to allocate dedicated transmission opportunity by Fast Ranging IE.
0b00: No indication. BS ignores this field (Default)
0b01: MS ranging parameters for Target BS, which is specified in this message are valid
0b10: MS has no valid ranging parameters for Target BS, which is specified in this message
0b11: Reserved
Target_BS_ID
Same as the Base Station ID parameter in the DL-MAP message of target BS.

\section*{Preamble Index/ Subchannel Index}

For the SCa and OFDMA PHY this parameter defines the PHY specific preamble for the target BS. For the OFDM PHY the 5 LSB contain the DL subchannel index (as defined in Table 211) used in the target BS sector. The 3 MSB shall be Reserved and set to 0 .

The MOB_HO-IND message shall include the following parameter encoded as TLV tuples:

\section*{HMAC/CMAC Tuple (See 11.1.2.)}

The HMAC/CMAC Tuple shall be the last attribute in the message.

\section*{Insert new subclause 6.3.2.3.56:}

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.56 BS Broadcast Paging (MOB_PAG-ADV) message}

The MOB_PAG-ADV message shall be sent on the Broadcast CID or Idle mode multicast CID during the BS Paging Interval.

The MAC Management Message Type for this message is given in Table 14. The format of the message is shown in Table 109p.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109p-BS Broadcast Paging (MOB_PAG-ADV) message format}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline MOB_PAG-ADV_Message_format() \{ & - & - \\
\hline Management Message Type=62 & 8 bits & - \\
\hline Num_Paging_Group_IDs & 16 bits & Number of Paging Group IDs in this message \\
\hline For ( $\mathrm{i}=0$; $\mathrm{i}<$ Num_Paging_Group_IDs; $\mathrm{i}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline Paging Group ID & 16 bits & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline Num_MACs & 8 bits & Number of MS MAC addresses \\
\hline For ( $\mathrm{j}=0 ; \mathrm{j}<$ Num_MACs; $\mathrm{j}++$ ) \{ & - & - \\
\hline MS MAC Address hash & 24 bits & The hash is obtained by computing a CRC24 on the MS 48-bit MAC address. The polynomial for the calculation is 0x1864CFB \\
\hline Action Code & 2 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
Paging action instruction to MS 0b00=No Action Required \\
0b01=Perform Ranging to establish location and acknowledge message \\
0b10=Enter Network \\
0b11=Reserved
\end{tabular} \\
\hline Reserved & 6 bits & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline padding & variable & Padding bits to ensure octet aligned \\
\hline TLV Encoded Information & variable & TLV specific \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

A BS shall generate MOB_PAG-ADV including the following parameters:

\section*{Paging Group ID ( $\mathbf{1 6 ~ b i t}$ )}

One or more logical affiliation groupings of BS.

\section*{MS MAC Address hash}

This is a 24-bit field used to hash the MS 48-bit MAC address. The hash value shall be the remainder of the division (Modulo 2) of the 48-bit MAC address multiplied by the polynomial D24 with the generator polynomial
$$
\begin{aligned}
& g(D)=D^{24}+D^{23}+D^{18}+D^{17}+D^{14}+D^{11}+D^{10}+D^{7}+D^{6}+D^{5}+D^{4}+D^{3}+D+1 \quad(h e x=1864 \mathrm{cfb})(\text { Exam- } \\
& \text { ple: }[\text { MS } 48 \text {-bit MAC address }]=00: D 0: 59: 0 \mathrm{~F}: E 2: 2 E, \text { hash should then be set to } 0 \times 51 \text { efe } 3)
\end{aligned}
$$

\section*{Action Code}

Paging action instruction to the MS to perform the following action:
0b00=No Action Required
0b01=Perform Ranging to establish location and acknowledge message
0b10=Enter Network
0b11=Reserved
For OFDMA PHY, when a BS pages multiple MSs, the BS may assign dedicated CDMA codes to one or more MS being paged. The BS shall first list the MAC Address Hash of those MSs that are assigned dedicated CDMA codes, followed by the MSs that are not assigned dedicated CDMA codes.

For OFDMA PHY, the following TLV may be included in the MOB_PAG-ADV management message:

\section*{CDMA code and transmission opportunity assignment (11.17.1)}

OFDMA-PHY specific parameter used to indicate CDMA code and transmission opportunity assigned to one or more MSs being paged in this message. One CDMA code and transmission opportunity assignment in the TLV corresponds to one MS paged. The order of the assignments is the same as the order of appearance of MS MAC address hash except with action code "No Action Required" in this message.

For OFDMA PHY, the following TLV may be included in the MOB_PAG-ADV management message. If a CDMA code assignment TLV is included, the Page-Response window TLV shall be included. There shall be no more than one occurrence of the Page-Response window TLV.

\section*{Page-Response window (11.17.2)}

OFDMA-PHY specific parameter used to indicate the time window (in unit of frames) during which the MS shall transmit the CDMA code at the transmission opportunity assigned in the CDMA code and transmission opportunity assignment TLV. The start of the window is the next frame after receiving the MOB_PAG-ADV.

\section*{Insert new subclause 6.3.2.3.56:}

\subsection*{6.3.2.3.57 Multicast Broadcast Service Map (MBS_MAP) message}

The BS may send an MBS_MAP message on an MBS portion to describe the MBS connections serviced by the MBS portion. When a MBS_MAP is sent, the connections need be described in the DL-MAP, but a MBS_MAP_IE() shall be substituted instead. The MBS MAP message format is presented in Table 109q. This message includes the MBS_DATA_IE and Extended_MBS_DATA_IE, which define the access information for the downlink and uplink MBS burst.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109q-MBS_MAP}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline MBS_MAP Message format ()\{ & - & - \\
\hline Management Message Type = & 4 bits & 62 \\
\hline Frame number & 4 bits & The frame number is identical to the 4 LSBs of the frame number in the DL-MAP \\
\hline MBS_DIUC_Change_Count & 8 bits & - \\
\hline \#MBS_DATA_IE & 4 bits & The number of included MBS_DATA_IE \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109q-MBS_MAP (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline For ( $\mathrm{i}=0 ; \mathrm{i}<n ; \mathrm{i}++$ )\{ & - & $n=\# \mathrm{MBS}$ _DATA_IE \\
\hline MBS_DATA_IE & variable & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline \#Extended_MBS_DATA_IE & - & \begin{tabular}{l}
The number of included \\
Extended_MBS_DATA_IE
\end{tabular} \\
\hline For(i=0;i<n;i++) \{ & - & $n=\#$ Extended_MBS_DATA_IE \\
\hline Extended_MBS_DATA_IE() & variable & - \\
\hline \} & & - \\
\hline \#MBS_DATA_Time_Diversity_IE & 4 bits & The number of included MBS_DATA_Time_Diversity_IE \\
\hline For $(\mathrm{i}=0 ; \mathrm{i}<m ; \mathrm{i}++)\{$ & - & $m=\# \mathrm{MBS}$ _DATA_Time diversity_IE \\
\hline MBS_DATA_Time_Diversity_IE() & variable & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline If(!byte boundary)\{ & - & - \\
\hline Padding Nibble & 4 bits & - \\
\hline \} & & - \\
\hline TLV encoding element & - & - \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\section*{MBS DIUC Change Count}

It is used to notify the Burst Profile used for Multi-BS-MBS data has been changed. If MBS_DIUC_Change_Count change, MS should wait until receiving DCD message unless Downlink Burst Profile TLV is included in MBS_MAP message.

The following TLV may be included in MBS_MAP message.

\section*{Downlink Burst Profile}

Downlink Burst Profile is used for the definition of MBS DIUC.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109r-MBS_MAP_Type}
\begin{tabular}{|c|l|}
\hline MBS_MAP_Type & \multicolumn{1}{|c|}{ Description } \\
\hline 0 & MBS_DATA_IE \\
\hline 1 & MBS_DATAA_Time_Diversity_IE \\
\hline 2 & Extended MBS_DATAA_IE \\
\hline $3 \sim 255$ & Reserved \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 109s-MBS_DATA_IE}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Syntax & Size & Notes \\
\hline MBS_DATA_IE\{ & - & - \\
\hline MBS_MAP Type $=0$ & 2 bits & MBS_DATA_IE \\
\hline MBS Burst Frame Offset & 2 bits & This indicates the burst located by this IE will be shown after MBS Burst Frame Offset + 2 frames. \\
\hline Next MBS MAP change indication & 1 bit & This indicates whether the size of MBS MAP message of next MB S frame for these Multicast CIDs included this IE will be different from the size of this MBS MAP message. \\
\hline No. of Multicast CID & 3 bits & - \\
\hline For(i=0; i< No. of Multicast CID; i++)\{ & - & - \\
\hline Multicast CID & 12 bits & 12 LSBs of CID for multicast. \\
\hline \} & - & - \\
\hline MBS DIUC & 4 bits & - \\
\hline OFDMA Symbol Offset & 8 bits & OFDMA symbol offset with respect to start of next (MBS Burst Frame offset + 2)th frame. \\
\hline Subchannel offset & 6 bits & OFDMA subchannel offset with respect to start of the next (MBS Burst Frame offset +2)th frame. \\
\hline Boosting & 3 bits & Refer to Table 273. \\
\hline No. OFDMA Symbols & 7 bits & The size of MBS data. \\
\hline No. Subchannels & 6 bits & - \\
\hline Repetition Coding Indication & 2 bits & \begin{tabular}{l}
